recipes.title: Tuscan chicken tray bake
recipes.description: A vibrant all-in-one dinner.

ingredients.name: eschallot or small onion, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 1 eschallot or small onion, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: chicken marylands, cut into drumstick and thigh pieces
ingredients.raw: 4 chicken marylands, cut into drumstick and thigh pieces

ingredients.name: basil leaves, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: ½ cup basil leaves, plus extra to serve

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced). In a large shallow baking dish, combine canned tomatoes, potatoes, eschallot, garlic, sundried tomatoes, Italian herbs, balsamic and sugar; mix to combine.
Place the chicken on top, mix to coat. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper. Roast for 40 minutes, turning chicken occasionally, or until the potatoes and chicken are browned and cooked.
Meanwhile, rinse the silverbeet under cold water to remove any dirt or grit. Drain and pat dry with kitchen paper. Use a small knife to remove the white stalks. Roughly chop the silverbeet.

Remove the chicken from the baking dish. Add the silverbeet, cream and basil leaves and toss to combine. Return the chicken to the dish and roast for a further 20 minutes, or until the silverbeet is tender.
Serve Tuscan chicken with extra basil leaves.

recipes.title: Chicken stew with baharat spice
recipes.description: An aromatic and hearty dinner idea.

ingredients.name: wedges preserved lemon , pulp removed, rind sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 2 wedges preserved lemon (80g), pulp removed, rind sliced thinly

ingredients.name: fresh flat-leaf parsley, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: ½ cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, chopped coarsely

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Combine baharat, chillies, honey and 1 tablespoon of the oil in a large bowl. Add chicken; turn to coat well. Season.
Heat remaining oil in a large flameproof casserole dish over medium heat. Cook chicken, skin-side down, for 4 minutes, turning, or until just starting to brown. Remove from dish.

Add onion and garlic to dish with ¼ cup (60ml) water; cook, stirring often, for 4 minutes or until onion is soft. Add stock, tomatoes and chicken with any juices. Cover dish, place in oven; cook for 30 minutes. Add potatoes; return to oven, covered, for a further 30 minutes or until chicken and potatoes are tender. Stand chicken stew for 10 minutes.
To serve, top chicken stew with preserved lemon rind, parsley and pistachios, season with salt; serve with yoghurt.

recipes.title: One pan creamy chicken, bacon & pumpkin
recipes.description: An easy dinner winner.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (60ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: medium brown onion, sliced thinly into wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (200g) brown onion, sliced thinly into wedges

ingredients.name: butternut pumpkin, peeled, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 500g butternut pumpkin, peeled, sliced thinly (1cm thick)

steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a large deep-sided frying pan over medium heat. Add bacon and cook for 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.

Dust chicken in seasoned flour. Increase heat to high. Add another tablespoon of oil to the pan, cook chicken for 5 minutes each side or until golden; remove from pan. Add the remaining oil to the pan, add the onion, garlic and pumpkin and cook stirring for 3-5 minutes, or until just starting to colour.
Add the cream, mustard, 1 ½ cups water and crumble in the stock cube. Bring to a simmer and cook, uncovered, for 8-10 minutes, or until pumpkin is tender.
Add balsamic and stir to combine. Return creamy chicken and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until heated through. Scatter with the cooked bacon and oregano leaves.

MASHED POTATO Meanwhile, place potatoes in a medium saucepan, cover with cold water. Bring to the boil, cook, uncovered, over medium heat, for 15 minutes or until potato is tender; drain then return to the saucepan and mash with a potato masher. Meanwhile, add the milk and butter to a separate pan, heat until the milk is hot and the butter has melted. Stir into the potato and, using a spatula or whisk, mix until smooth. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Serve creamy chicken with mashed potato.

recipes.title: Steph de Sousa’s one pan chicken risoni
recipes.description: Fall in love with my risoni twist on the viral Marry Me Chicken recipe.

ingredients.name: jar sun-dried tomato pesto
ingredients.raw: 190g jar sun-dried tomato pesto (or sun-dried tomato strips)

steps: Season your chicken with a pinch of salt and pepper. Heat a splash of olive oil in a medium frying pan over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken on both sides to create a tasty deep brown crust. Don’t worry if it’s not cooked through yet; we’ll get there!
Remove the chicken from the pan, then add the diced onion for a quick fry. Let them soak up all that flavour left behind by the chicken.
Stir in the tomato pesto, risoni, stock and cream. Watch as it all starts to bubble away with joy!
Return the chicken to the pan, turn the heat down to low, cover partially with a lid and let it simmer away for 15 minutes.

Remove the lid, add the green beans for their moment of glory and simmer for 5 minutes. Turn off the heat, pop the lid back on and relax for 5 minutes—you are looking for all that delicious liquid to be absorbed, the risoni to be perfectly al dente and your chicken cooked to perfection.
Serve up this creamy, dreamy dish and watch everyone fall head over heels for your cooking!

recipes.title: One pan layered burrito bake
recipes.description: Fall in love with this burrito-bowl-meets-lasagne dinner.

ingredients.name: x 300g jars super smoky chipotle salsa
ingredients.raw: 2 x 300g jars super smoky chipotle salsa

ingredients.name: sour cream, hot sauce and lime wedges, to serve
ingredients.raw: sour cream, hot sauce and lime wedges, to serve

steps: Finely chop the onion and garlic. Pick the coriander leaves and reserve; finely chop roots and stems. Rinse and drain kidney beans. Juice the lime. Mash avocado in a small bowl; stir in the lime juice and season with sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan forced).

Heat oil in the casserole pan over medium-high heat. Cook onion, garlic and coriander roots and stems for 3 minutes or until onion is softened. Add beef; cook, breaking up with a wooden spoon, for 5 minutes or until browned. Add spice blend, passata and beans; stir until well combined then simmer for five minutes. Transfer to a large bowl.

Place a burrito wrap on the base of the casserole. Spoon over a third of the meat mixture, a third of the salsa, a quarter of the reserved coriander leaves and a third of the cheese. Repeat layering twice more, finishing with the cheese. Bake for 30 minutes or until top is golden. Stand for 5 minutes before slicing.
Top burrito bake with mashed avocado, sour cream, hot sauce and remaining coriander leaves. Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s one-pan char siu chicken
recipes.description: The whole family will love the gorgeous sticky and sweet flavours of this easy char siu.

ingredients.name: bone-in, skin-on chicken thigh cutlets, excess fat trimmed
ingredients.raw: 6 bone-in, skin-on chicken thigh cutlets, excess fat trimmed

ingredients.name: rice wine vinegar
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (60ml) rice wine (or white) vinegar

ingredients.name: chinese broccoli, trimmed and cut into thirds lengthways
ingredients.raw: 1 bunch Chinese broccoli, trimmed and cut into thirds lengthways

steps: In a large high-sided frying pan over medium-high heat, place the sesame seeds and toast until they are golden. Remove to a bowl and wipe out the pan.

Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towel. Add the oil to the same pan over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, place the chicken skin-side down and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until the skin is deep golden brown. Turn and cook for a further 5 minutes.

In a medium bowl combine the char siu sauce, soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, garlic and ginger. Pour the sauce mixture into the pan. Cook, uncovered, over medium heat, spooning the sauce mixture over the chicken occasionally, for 15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through. If the sauce starts to thicken and reduce too much before the chicken is fully cooked, add a little water to the pan.

Meanwhile, place the noodles and the hard stem parts of the broccoli into a large heatproof bowl and cover with boiling water from the kettle. After about one minute add the rest of the broccoli leaves and the green onion to the water to blanch. After one minute further, strain the noodles and vegetables and nestle around the chicken in the pan.
To serve the char siu chicken, sprinkle with the toasted sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s Spanish chicken
recipes.description: A delicious one pan dinner.

ingredients.name: large red capsicum, quartered, seeded and pith removed
ingredients.raw: 1 large red capsicum, quartered, seeded and pith removed

ingredients.name: ripe medium truss tomatoes
ingredients.raw: 4 ripe medium truss tomatoes (or half a can of tomatoes)

ingredients.name: large brown onion, peeled and quartered
ingredients.raw: 1 large brown onion, peeled and quartered

ingredients.name: chorizo, cut into halves lengthwise then 1cm chunks
ingredients.raw: 1 chorizo, cut into halves lengthwise then 1cm chunks

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: chicken thigh cutlets, excess fat trimmed
ingredients.raw: 6 chicken thigh cutlets, excess fat trimmed

steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C fan-forced.
Place the capsicum, tomatoes, onion, garlic, and the roots and stems of the coriander into a blender or food processor and process until smooth.

Place a large chef's pan with oven-proof handle over medium-high heat. Sauté the chorizo until golden. Add half of the olive oil and the pureed vegetable mixture and stir until the mixture comes to the boil and reduces slightly. Add the olives, half of the salt, pepper and half the paprika. Cook until the mixture is rich and thickened. Remove the pan from the heat.

Place the potato slices into the pan and turn them to coat with the sauce. Nestle the chicken pieces skin-side up around the pan. Drizzle the entire dish with the remaining oil, and sprinkle the skin of the chicken with the rest of the salt and paprika.
Place the pan into the oven and bake for 45-50 minutes, or until the skin of the chicken brown and crisp, and the potatoes cooked through.

Remove the pan from the oven and scatter the coriander leaves over the top and place the lemon wedges around the sides. Serve to the table in the pan allowing everyone to help themselves. Serve Spanish chicken with a crisp green salad, if you like.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s Greek-style chicken tray bake
recipes.description: An easy family feast.

ingredients.name: dutch cream or desiree potatoes, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 800g Dutch cream or desiree potatoes, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (60ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: bulbs garlic, top chopped off to reveal the tips of the cloves
ingredients.raw: 2 bulbs garlic, top chopped off to reveal the tips of the cloves

steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).

Spread the potatoes over the base of a medium heavy-based baking dish; pour stock over potatoes. Arrange chicken over potatoes; drizzle chicken with oil, then sprinkle with oregano and rind, and season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Nestle the onions around the chicken and bake for 20 minutes.

Remove the dish from the oven. Drizzle with the lemon juice and add garlic; return to the oven and roast for a further 15 minutes. Add tomatoes and olives. Roast for a further 5-10 minutes or until the Greek chicken is cooked through and the potatoes and garlic are tender and browned. If potatoes need more browning, transfer the chicken to a dish; cover to keep warm. Then, return potato to the oven for about 10 minutes or until browned.
Crumble fetta over the Greek chicken. Serve with a green salad. Diners can squeeze the garlic out of its skin to serve with the chicken.

recipes.title: Chilli con carne
recipes.description: This spicy Mexican beef and bean stew is a real crowd-pleaser that the whole family will love.

ingredients.name: long green chillies, seeded, finely chopped
ingredients.raw: 3 long green chillies, seeded, finely chopped

ingredients.name: or to taste
ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoon chilli powder, or to taste

ingredients.name: can red kidney beans, drained, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 400g can red kidney beans, drained, rinsed

ingredients.name: steamed rice, sour cream and lime wedges, for serving
ingredients.raw: steamed rice, sour cream and lime wedges, for serving

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/160°C fan-forced. Heat half the oil in a medium flameproof heavy-based pan over high heat. Cook mince, stirring, to break up lumps, until browned. Remove mince from pan.
Lower the heat to medium; add remaining oil and cook onion, garlic, chillies and spices until the onion is soft.

Return mince to pan; stir in tomato paste, tomatoes and kidney beans. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Stir in stock then bake, covered, for 45 minutes. Top with coriander and serve with rice, sour cream and lime wedges.

recipes.title: Creamy lemon chicken
recipes.description: A delicious 30 minute dinner cooked in one pan.

ingredients.name: plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (40g) finely grated parmesan, plus extra to serve

steps: Combine smoked paprika and flour in a shallow bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Add chicken breasts and dust well to coat.
Heat butter and oil in a large frying pan over medium high heat. Cook the chicken for 3 minutes each side or until golden, but not yet cooked all the way through. Remove and set aside.

Add the garlic and cook, stirring, until just fragrant. Add the stock, cream and green beans and bring to a simmer. Return chicken to the pan. Reduce heatto medium low; cook for 8-10 minutes, or until chicken is tender and cooked through.
Remove the chicken and add the spinach, parmesan and lemon rind and juice; cook, stirring until spinach starts to wilt. Return the chicken and serve.

recipes.title: Greek-style lemon chicken rice with fetta
recipes.description: A hearty one pan family meal.

ingredients.name: pitted kalamata olives, halved
ingredients.raw: ¾ cup (120g) pitted kalamata olives, halved

ingredients.name: fresh oregano leaves, to serve
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons fresh oregano leaves, to serve

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (160° fan-forced). Combine chicken, dried oregano, lemon rind and garlic in a small bowl. Toss well to coat and set aside to marinate for 10 minutes or overnight if you have time.
Heat oil in a large shallow casserole pan over medium heat. Add chicken and cook for 4 minutes each side, until golden. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
Add onion and cook for 6-8 minutes, stirring until softened and golden. Add the rice and stir to combine, then the stock and lemon juice. Place chicken back on top of the rice.
Bring to a simmer, cover with a lid. Place in the oven and bake for 30 minutes, until rice is tender. Set aside, covered, for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle over the olives and oregano and fluff with a fork.

recipes.title: Chicken cacciatore
recipes.description: Warm up with this hearty favourite.

ingredients.name: kilogram chicken thigh cutlets, skin on
ingredients.raw: 1.5 kilogram (3 pounds) chicken thigh cutlets, skin on

ingredients.name: chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (60g) seeded black olives, chopped coarsely
1/2 cup coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Heat half the oil in large saucepan; cook chicken, in batches, until browned. Remove from pan.
Heat remaining oil in same pan; cook onion and garlic, stirring, until onion softens. Stir in wine, vinegar, stock, undrained tomatoes, paste and anchovies.

Return chicken to pan, fitting pieces tightly together in a single layer; bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, covered, 20 minutes. Uncover; simmer about 30 minutes or until chicken is tender and sauce is reduced. Skim fat from surface; stir in olives and parsley, season to taste.

recipes.title: Apricot chicken tray bake
recipes.description: A deliciously sticky, sweet and savoury one-pan dinner.

ingredients.name: large red onion, cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 large (300g) red onion, cut into wedges

ingredients.name: coriander sprigs, to serve
ingredients.raw: ½ cup coriander sprigs, to serve

ingredients.name: steamed rice, broccolini, optional, to serve
ingredients.raw: Steamed rice, broccolini, optional, to serve

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan forced). Grease and line a large roasting dish with baking paper.

Combine apricot nectar, kecap manis, honey, ginger, garlic and chilli flakes in the roasting dish. Add the chicken and toss to coat. Tuck the onion, star anise, cinnamon and apricots amongst the chicken mixture. Roast for 45 minutes, basting and turning the chicken twice during cooking until chicken is browned and cooked through.
Serve topped with coriander sprigs and accompany with steamed rice and broccolini.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s one pan chicken pilaf
recipes.description: A spiced chicken and rice dinner.

ingredients.name: chicken thigh fillets, cut into 2cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 600g chicken thigh fillets, cut into 2cm pieces

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup loosely packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Heat a deep large frying pan over medium-high heat; add the almonds. Cook, stirring, until the almonds are light golden brown. Remove from pan.

Add 3 teaspoons of the oil to hot pan. Cook half the chicken over high heat, stirring, for 3 minutes or until golden; transfer to a bowl. Repeat with another 3 teaspoons of oil and remaining chicken; place in bowl.

Heat the remaining oil to the pan over medium heat; cook the onion and garlic, stirring, for 5 minutes until soft and fragrant. Add the curry leaves and mustard seeds; cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until the seeds are fragrant and start to pop. Add the remaining spices and rice; cook, stirring, for a further 1 minute.

Stir in the diced tomatoes and chicken stock; bring to the boil. Stir in the chicken; reduce the heat to medium-low. Cook, covered, for 15 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is just tender. You may need to stir constantly in the last few minutes as the pilaf dries out. The dish is ready when it is quite dry. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Scatter with the almonds and coriander leaves; serve with yoghurt.

recipes.title: One pot salmon and rice
recipes.description: What’s not to love about a one-pot meal?  This easy pilaf will have dinner on the table in 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup (55g) roasted almond kernels, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.raw: ½ cup loosely packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or deep-frying pan over medium heat; cook onion and garlic, stirring, for 5 minutes or until softened but not coloured.
Add rice to pan; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes or until grains appear transparent. Stir in stock; bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer, covered, for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine spices in a medium bowl. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Add salmon, toss to coat in spice mixture.
Place salmon and broccoli on rice; cook, covered, for a further 3 minutes. Remove pan from heat; stand for 5 minutes or until broccoli and rice are tender and stock is absorbed.
Flake salmon into pieces. Use a fork to separate the rice grains. Top the pilaf with the almonds and coriander. Serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: One pan mac and cheese
recipes.description: Mac and cheese cooked all in one pan? It doesn't get much easier than that for dinner, which is exactly what you want when camping.

ingredients.name: finely grated parmesan, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (40g) finely grated parmesan, plus extra to serve

steps: Place the water, macaroni, salt and butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Cook, stirring, until mixture comes to the boil. Reduce heat to low-medium; cook, stirring mixture continuously, for 8 minutes or until pasta is just cooked through and liquid is thickened and reduced (the butter and starch from the pasta will thicken the pasta cooking water). If the pasta starts to catch in the corner or the mixture becomes too dry before cooking through, add a few extra tablespoons of water.
Stir in the cream, mustard and cheeses until cheeses melt. Fold in peas and cavolo nero until leaves have wilted. Season to taste and top with extra parmesan to serve.

recipes.title: One pot pasta
recipes.description: A fresh and easy spaghetti that cooks in just 15 minutes!
steps: Cut onion into thin wedges. Thinly slice garlic and capsicum. Halve cherry tomatoes. Drain bocconcini. Reserve 2 sprigs of basil and pick remaining leaves

Place onion, garlic, capsicum, fresh and canned tomatoes, bocconcini, capers, olives, spaghetti and sea salt in the casserole. Pour over 3 cups (750ml) water, the cream and half the oil. Season with freshly ground black pepper. Cover, then bring to the boil over high heat, stirring occasionally to ensure pasta is covered with liquid. Reduce heat to medium-high. Cook, uncovered, stirring frequently, for 10 minutes or until pasta is al dente and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Serve spaghetti topped with parmesan and reserved basil sprigs; drizzle with remaining oil and season with pepper.

recipes.title: One pot chicken and rice
recipes.description: This mild and simple recipe is perfect for fussy eaters.
steps: In a large saucepan, heat oil on high. Saute onion 2-3 minutes until tender. Add chicken and cook, stirring, 2-3 minutes until well browned.
Stir rice, tomatoes, water, zucchini and herbs through. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to low. Simmer, covered, 15-20 minutes, until rice is tender.
Set pan aside, covered, 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Stir parsley through and season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with salad and crusty bread if liked.

recipes.title: Honey mustard chicken tray bake
recipes.description: Serve with buttered carrots.
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Heat half the oil in a flameproof casserole dish over medium-high heat, add chicken, skin-side down; cook, turning occasionally, for 6 minutes or until browned. Transfer to a plate.

Add remaining oil to casserole dish, add onion and garlic; cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes or until softened. Deglaze pan with cider, then stir in stock, mustard, honey and rosemary; bring to the boil. Return chicken, skin-side up, to dish; season, cover with a lid or foil and cook in the oven for 15 minutes or until chicken is cooked through. Transfer chicken to a tray and discard rosemary.
Add the crème fraîche and vinegar to casserole dish, whisk to combine; season to taste. Return the chicken to sauce.

Meanwhile, make the buttered carrots: Trim carrots and reserve 2 tablespoons carrot tops. Scrub carrots then cut any large carrots in half lengthways. Cook carrots in a saucepan of boiling salted water for 3 minutes or until just tender. Drain, then toss in a bowl with remaining ingredients and reserved carrot tops; season to taste.
Serve chicken with buttered carrots.

recipes.title: One pan red curry pork cutlets
recipes.description: A flavour-packed one pan wonder.

ingredients.name: thai basil and roti canai, to serve
ingredients.raw: Thai basil and roti canai, to serve

steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).
Peel and cut pumpkin into 1cm thick slices. Slice eggplant into 1cm thick rounds. Peel eschalots and cut in half. Scrunch lime leaves.

Place curry paste, 1/2 cup (125ml) coconut milk, the fish sauce, palm sugar and lime juice in a large bowl; stir to combine. Transfer half the marinade to the baking dish, then stir into the bowl the remaining coconut milk, coconut cream and lime leaves. Add pork cutlets to bowl and turn to coat well in the marinade; season with freshly ground black pepper.
Place the dish in the oven for 10 minutes or until sauce is bubbling. Add pumpkin, eggplant and eschalots to dish; turn to coat in the sauce. Place pork on top.
Cover with foil and roast for 20 minutes.
Remove foil and baste pork with sauce; place tomatoes on top. Increase heat to 220°C (200°C fan-forced), roast, uncovered, for a further 10 minutes until pork is cooked and vegetables are tender.
Top curry with Thai basil and serve with roti.

recipes.title: Baked gnocchi and meatballs
recipes.description: The whole family will love this quick and easy gnocchi and meatball tray bake.
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F.
Chop sausages coarsely. Combine sausages and oil in shallow flameproof dish (19cm x 28cm). Bake, uncovered, about 20 minutes or until sausages are browned and have burst into little meatballs.
Meanwhile, halve olives. Add olives, gnocchi, sauce and oregano to dish; season. Sprinkle with cheese.
Bake, uncovered, about 25 minutes or until gnocchi is tender.

recipes.title: Pumpkin & sage gnocchi pasta bake
recipes.description: Take your weeknight pasta bake to a new level.
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C/200°C fan.
Cut pumpkin into 2cm thick wedges and place in the baking dish with the gnocchi.

Microwave garlic butter in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second bursts until melted. Drizzle melted butter over gnocchi and pumpkin; toss to coat. Pour combined stock and cream over gnocchi and pumpkin; dot with large chunks of ricotta. Season with sea salt and freshly ground black pepper. Bake for 30 minutes or until pumpkin is almost tender. Reduce oven to 200°C/180°C fan.

Meanwhile, pick sage leaves and place in a small bowl; pour over oil and toss to coat. Once the gnocchi has been cooking for 30 minutes, add pine nuts and sage leaves to dish; reserve sage oil. Bake for a further 10 minutes or until sage leaves are crisp, pine nuts are golden and pumpkin is cooked through.
Drizzle gnocchi with reserved sage oil and serve.

recipes.title: Creamy one pan chicken with corn
recipes.description: A flavour-packed weeknight dinner, on the table in 25 minutes.
steps: Using a meat tenderiser or rolling pin, beat chicken breasts between 2 sheets of baking paper to flatten slightly.
In a shallow bowl, combine flour and lemon rind. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Press flour into all sides of chicken.

Heat oil and half the butter in a medium frying pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook for about 5 minutes on the first side or until golden brown and a crust has developed. Turn; cook for a further 3 minutes or until chicken is just cooked through. Remove chicken from pan.

Add remaining butter, garlic and the corn kernels to the pan, cook for 2 minute or until fragrant. Add stock and cream. Cook, uncovered until sauce has thickened. Return chicken and any juices to pan, simmer, for 2 minutes. Stir in chives.
Sprinkle with basil leaves.

recipes.title: Chicken & potato tray bake
recipes.description: It's big on flavour and light on washing up.

ingredients.name: medium sebago potatoes, scrubbed, chopped into 3cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 3 medium (600g) Sebago potatoes, scrubbed, chopped into 3cm pieces

ingredients.name: each ground cumin and ground coriander
ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon each ground cumin and ground coriander

ingredients.name: fresh curry leaves, roti, natural yoghurt, eggplant pickle or mango chutney, for serving
ingredients.raw: 3 sprigs fresh curry leaves, roti, natural yoghurt, eggplant pickle or mango chutney, for serving

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced). Line a large shallow baking dish with baking paper.

Combine the butter, potato, onion, ginger, garlic, garam marsala, cumin, coriander, coriander stalks, lemon juice and chicken in the baking dish. Mix well so all ingredients are coated in the spices. Nestle the tomato wedges among the other ingredients. Season liberally with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Roast for 1 hour until chicken is golden and cooked through and potatoes are tender. Add the curry leaves and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Serve with warm roti, yoghurt and chutney.

recipes.title: Spicy zucchini & ricotta stuffed pasta shells
recipes.description: Why make dinner more complicated than it needs to be? This pasta is perfect for weeknights and make tasty leftovers for lunch. If you’re not a fan of chilli, just leave out the chilli flakes.

ingredients.name: extra grated parmesan, green salad, to serve
ingredients.raw: extra grated parmesan, green salad, to serve

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced). Oil a shallow 2.5-litre (10-cup) ovenproof dish.
Combine the zucchini, ricotta, parmesan, pine nuts, egg yolks, garlic, basil, thyme and chilli in a medium bowl; season.
Combine pasta sauce and water and spread into dish; season. Spoon zucchini mixture into uncooked pasta shells; place in dish.

Cover dish with greased foil; bake for 40 minutes. Uncover; bake for about 15 minutes or until pasta is tender and cheese is browned lightly. Serve pasta sprinkled with extra thyme leaves and parmesan. Serve with a green salad.

recipes.title: Cranberry-glazed lamb chop tray bake
recipes.description: Sticky, sweet and perfect for a weeknight meal.

ingredients.name: lamb forequarter chops, trim excess fat
ingredients.raw: 8 (1.5kg) lamb forequarter chops, trim excess fat

ingredients.name: large onions, peeled, cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 2 large (400g) onions, peeled, cut into wedges

ingredients.name: dry red wine or beef stock
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (125ml) dry red wine or beef stock

steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced). Place flour on a plate, season with salt and freshly ground pepper and stir to combine. Coat lamb in flour mixture; shake off excess.

Heat oil in a large frying pan, cook the lamb, in batches, for 5 minutes on each side or until well browned. Transfer to a large baking dish. Add onions to the pan, cook for 2 minutes or until lightly browned both sides. Add to baking dish with the garlic cloves.
Whisk cranberry sauce, mustard, wine and ¾ cup (180ml) water in a large jug until combined. Pour over lamb mixture.
Cover baking dish tightly with foil and roast for 1 hour. Remove foil, baste lamb and onions with pan juices and roast, uncovered, for a further 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Serve lamb with steamed green beans, if desired.

recipes.title: Chicken nibbles with chilli crunch potatoes
recipes.description: Serve a spicy or mild version.

ingredients.name: medium potatoes, peeled, halved or quartered
ingredients.raw: 6 medium (1.2kg) potatoes, peeled, halved or quartered

ingredients.name: green salad for serving, if desired
ingredients.raw: Green salad for serving, if desired

steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced). Boil, steam or microwave potatoes until almost cooked through; drain. Cool for 5 minutes then gently rake rounded side of potatoes with the tines of a fork. Place, cut-side down, in a single layer, on a large oven tray lined with baking paper.
If the tray is large enough, add the chicken nibbles to the tray. (Alternatively use two medium oven trays.)

Combine olive oil, garlic cloves and rosemary sprigs in a small bowl. Brush potatoes and chicken liberally with oil mixture; add garlic and rosemary to tray. Dot with the butter and season liberally with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Roast for 50 minutes, turning and basting twice during cooking, until potatoes and chicken are browned and crisp.
CHILLI CRUNCH Meanwhile, combine garlic, pepper, chilli flakes and sesame seeds in a bowl. Heat olive oil in a small saucepan until hot; immediately pour over the chilli mixture.
Serve potatoes and chicken drizzled with Chilli Crunch.

recipes.title: One pot Korean baked chicken, rice & kimchi
recipes.description: Your new favourite chicken rice!

ingredients.name: korean soy bean paste
ingredients.raw: 2 tbsp Korean soy bean paste (see tip)

ingredients.name: gochujang paste
ingredients.raw: 1 tbsp gochujang paste (Korean red chilli paste)

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C/180°C fan.

Remove stalks from mushrooms and discard; slice mushrooms. Cut eggplant into 2cm pieces. Drain kimchi; reserve ¼ cup (60ml) liquid. Cut wombok into thin wedges. Finely grate ginger. Crush garlic. Thinly slice 3 green onions on an angle; thinly slice remaining green onion lengthways and place in a bowl of iced water to curl.

Place ginger, garlic, sliced green onion, soy bean paste, gochujang, soy sauce, kecap manis and oil in a large bowl; stir to combine. Add eggplant and chicken; toss to coat well. Rinse rice under cold running water until water runs clear.

Place rice, stock, ¼ cup (60ml) water and reserved kimchi liquid in the casserole. Arrange wombok, mushrooms, chicken and eggplant on top, ensuring chicken skin is exposed. Cover with the lid. Bake for 40 minutes.
Increase oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Top with drained kimchi and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Bake for a further 15 minutes, uncovered, or until chicken is golden, liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Serve chicken, vegetables and rice topped with drained green onion curls.

recipes.title: One-pot pasta with beef and capsicum
recipes.description: One-pot means less washing up!

ingredients.name: salt-reduced stock powder or gluten-free stock
ingredients.raw: 2 tsp salt-reduced stock powder or gluten-free stock

ingredients.name: wholemeal dried penne or gluten-free penne
ingredients.raw: 180g wholemeal dried penne or gluten-free penne

steps: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onions and cook, stirring often, for 10 minutes or until golden. Add the mince and cook, breaking apart any lumps with a wooden spoon, for 2-3 minutes or until the mince is browned. Stir in the capsicum and cook, stirring often, for 5 minutes.

Add the garlic, thyme and paprika to the pan. Stir well. Add the tomatoes, tomato paste and stock and bring to the boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes. Add the penne, put the lid back on and return to the boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for a further 12 minutes or until the penne is tender. Set aside to stand for 5 minutes.
Divide half the pasta between two serving plates and serve.

Leave the remainder of the pasta to cool. Transfer to an airtight container and keep in the fridge for up to 4 days. Reheat in a pan over a low heat, or gently in the microwave, with a splash of water until piping hot.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s lamb forequarter chops with vegetables tray bake
recipes.description: Keep cleaning-up to a minimum and flavour to a maximum with this one-dish dinner.

ingredients.name: large carrots, peeled, cut into 2cm slices
ingredients.raw: 3 large (550g) carrots, peeled, cut into 2cm slices

ingredients.name: medium potatoes, peeled, cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 3 medium (600g) potatoes, peeled, cut into wedges

ingredients.name: chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves, chopped coarsely

steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced). Dust the lamb lightly in seasoned flour.
Place onion slices, carrot and potato over the base of a large baking dish. Arrange the lamb over the vegetables, nestling the potatoes around the lamb.

Combine the sauces, mustard, garlic and sugar with ½ cup of water in a large jug; pour the sauce mixture over ingredients in dish. Cover the dish tightly with foil. Cook for 40 minutes. Remove foil; turn the lamb and spoon the liquid over the vegetables. Cook, uncovered, for a further 40 minutes, turning the lamb and basting occasionally.
Add the zucchini to the dish around the lamb. Cook, uncovered, for a further 20 minutes or until lamb is very tender and zucchini is just cooked.
Serve the lamb and vegetables scattered with parsley.

recipes.title: One-pan chicken rice with coconut
recipes.description: A delicious dinner for two in just under 30 minutes? Yes please!

ingredients.name: small red chillies
ingredients.raw: 2 small red chillies (see Test Kitchen tip)

ingredients.name: coriander with roots attached
ingredients.raw: ½ bunch coriander with roots attached

steps: Thinly slice chillies. Pick coriander leaves; finely chop roots and stems. Reserve coriander leaves and half the chillies to serve.

Pour coconut milk and stock into a 26cm-30cm shallow casserole or deep -frying pan; add garlic, ginger and lemongrass pastes, followed by coriander roots and stems and remaining chilli. Crush lime leaves in your hands and add to the pan. Bring to a simmer over medium heat; simmer gently for 8 minutes for flavours to develop.
Meanwhile, thinly slice chicken. Add chicken and rice to infused stock; cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, trim green beans and sugar snap peas. Cut beans in half on an angle.

Stir fish sauce, lime juice and sugar into pan. Stir in beans and sugar snap peas; cook, covered, for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally to release any rice stuck to the base of the pan, or until chicken and veggies are cooked through. Stir in spinach leaves, then adjust seasoning, adding more lime juice, fish sauce and sugar for balance.
Scatter coconut chicken and rice with reserved coriander leaves and chilli. Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Persian-style chicken tray bake with  pearl couscous
recipes.description: Try our simple, subtly spiced chicken tray bake dish sweetly studded with currants and pistachios.

ingredients.name: marinated pitted green olives, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (40g) marinated pitted green olives, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: each dill, mint, parsley, leaves picked
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup each dill, mint, parsley, leaves picked

ingredients.name: pomegranate molasses, optional, to serve
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon pomegranate molasses, optional, to serve

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced). Place the paprika, oil, honey, vinegar, garlic, onion, capsicum, currants and chicken skin side down in an oiled medium-sized baking dish. Season and toss to combine.

Roast uncovered for 40 minutes. Carefully remove the chicken, add the couscous and stock and stir to combine. Return the chicken skin side up and cook for 25-30 minutes, or until the couscous is tender and chicken is golden. Set aside to rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the pistachio, olives, herbs and lemon juice in a small bowl, toss to combine.
Sprinkle over the herb salad mixture. Serve drizzled with a little pomegranate molasses if desired.

recipes.title: Lemon parmesan pan chicken
recipes.description: Serve this delicious weeknight wonder with creamy mashed potato.

ingredients.name: finely grated lemon rind, plus extra for serving
ingredients.raw: finely grated lemon rind, plus extra for serving

steps: Using a meat tenderiser or rolling pin, beat chicken breasts between 2 sheets of baking paper to flatten slightly.
In a shallow bowl, combine flour, ½ cup of the parmesan and lemon rind. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Press flour into all sides of chicken.

Heat oil and half the butter in a medium frying pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook for about 5 minutes on the first side or until golden brown and a crust has developed. Turn; cook for a further 3 minutes or until chicken is just cooked through. Remove chicken from pan.

Add remaining butter and garlic to pan, cook for 1 minute or until fragrant. Add stock and cream. Cook, uncovered until sauce has thickened. Return chicken and any juices to pan, simmer, stirring, for 2 minutes.

Sprinkle with remaining parmesan, chives, basil and extra lemon rind. Serve with mashed potato and lemon wedges.
[jwp-video n="1"]

recipes.title: One-pot garlic prawns pilaf
recipes.description: Perfect any day of the week!

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil, plus extra for serving
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, plus extra for serving

ingredients.name: large green prawns, peeled and deveined, tails intact
ingredients.raw: 12 (700g) large green prawns, peeled and deveined, tails intact

ingredients.name: basmati rice, rinsed in cold water, drained
ingredients.raw: 2 cups (400g) basmati rice, rinsed in cold water, drained

ingredients.name: verjuice or white wine
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (125ml) verjuice or white wine

steps: Heat half the butter and oil in a large frying pan (or shallow casserole dish) over high heat. Add prawns, cook for 2 minutes on one side then turn, add half the garlic; cook for a further minute. Remove from pan; cover to keep warm.

Reduce the heat to medium, add remaining butter and onion to the pan, cook, stirring, until onion is soft and translucent. Add the rice; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes or until coated in the onion mixture. Stir in the verjuice, cook, stirring, until evaporated.
Increase the heat to high, stir in the stock and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer, covered, for about 12 minutes or until rice is tender and has absorbed all of the stock.
Remove from heat and stand, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff rice with fork and stir in the prawns, sprinkle with dill and serve with rocket and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Paprika lamb forequarter chops tray bake
recipes.description: A simple one-tray dinner ideal for busy weeknights.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup (80ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: grams cherry truss tomatoes, on the vine
ingredients.raw: 500 grams cherry truss tomatoes, on the vine

ingredients.name: loosely packed flat-leaf parsley leaves
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup loosely packed flat-leaf parsley leaves

steps: Preheat oven to 220C/200C fan. Line a baking dish with baking paper.
Combine lamb, turmeric, paprika, rind, garlic and half the oil in a prepared dish; season. Spread into a single layer; roast for 15 minutes.
Remove dish from oven, add tomatoes; drizzle with half the remaining oil, season. Return to oven; roast for 15 minutes or until lamb is cooked as desired.
Just before serving, add chickpeas, parsley, juice and remaining oil to dish. Serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Oven-baked risotto with chicken, rocket and tomato
recipes.description: A one-pan risotto recipe ideal for busy weeknights.

ingredients.name: coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
ingredients.raw: 1 tbsp coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Preheat the oven to 200C (180C fan-forced).

Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a large frying pan or flameproof baking dish to medium-high heat; cook onion and garlic, stirring, for 5 minutes or until onion softens. Add rice; stir to coat in onion mixture. Stir in the wine and then chicken stock; bring to the boil.

Season the chicken with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Place chicken, in a single layer, on top of the rice mixture; cover. Transfer to oven; bake for 25 minutes or until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through. Stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
Combine tomatoes with remaining oil and season with salt and freshly ground pepper. Sprinkle tomatoes, rocket, parmesan and parsley over the risotto.

recipes.title: Cheat’s minestrone with meatballs
recipes.description: This popular soup gets a makeover in the cheat's version with hearty meatballs. It's an economical and satisfying recipe.

ingredients.name: grams green beans, trimmed, sliced diagonally
ingredients.raw: 80 grams green beans, trimmed, sliced diagonally

ingredients.name: grams canned borlotti beans, drained, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 400 grams canned borlotti beans, drained, rinsed

ingredients.name: loosely packed small basil leaves
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup loosely packed small basil leaves

steps: Squeeze sausage meat from casings into a medium bowl, discard casings. Roll sausage meat into 2cm meatballs, using wet hands. Heat half the oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat; cook meatballs, turning, about 5 minutes or until browned all over. Remove from pan.
Heat remaining oil in same pan; cook onion and garlic, stirring, about 5 minutes or until onion softens. Add paste; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes. Add the water, stock and passata; bring to the boil.

Add celery to pan; simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes. Add carrot, zucchini and green beans; simmer, uncovered, about 20 minutes or until carrot is tender. Return meatballs to pan with borlotti beans and pasta; simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, about 10 minutes or until pasta is tender. Season to taste.
Serve soup topped with basil and parmesan. Sopressa, a salami from the north of Italy, can be found in both mild and chilli-flavoured varieties. If unavailable, you can use any hot salami.

recipes.title: Garlic butter fish and crispy potatoes
recipes.description: It's fast, fabulous and great for whipping up on a busy weeknight.

ingredients.name: large sweet potato, peeled, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 large (400g) sweet potato, peeled, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: medium red royal potatoes, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (600g) red royal potatoes, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup (80ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: grams store-bought garlic and herb butter, softened
ingredients.raw: 100 grams store-bought garlic and herb butter, softened

steps: Preheat oven to 240C (220C fan-forced). Line a large oven tray with baking paper.
Place the potatoes in a bowl. Add oil, season with salt and freshly ground black pepper, and toss to combine. Place in a single layer on the prepared tray. Bake for 25 minutes, or until golden.
Meanwhile, combine the butter and capers in a small bowl.
Remove the potato mixture from the oven and push them up one end of the oven tray. Arrange fish fillets on the same tray and spread the butter mixture evenly over each fillet.
Return to the oven and bake for another 10 minutes, or until fish is just cooked through.
Serve the fish with potatoes and salad leaves.

recipes.title: Osso buco with gremolata
recipes.description: Warm up with this classic comforting osso buco oven recipe.
steps: Preheat oven to 170°C/340°F.
Coat veal in flour; shake off excess. Heat oil in large flameproof casserole dish over high heat; cook veal, in batches, for 2 minutes each side or until browned. Remove from pan.

Cook onion in same dish, stirring over medium-high heat, for 5 minutes or until softened. Add garlic and wine; bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, until wine is reduced by half. Add passata and stock; return veal to pan, spoon sauce mixture over veal. Bring to the boil. Cover with a lid or foil. Cook in oven for 2 hours or until the veal is almost falling off the bone.
Meanwhile, make gremolata: Using a zester, remove rind from lemon into thin strips (or finely grate). Combine rind, parsley and garlic in a small bowl.
Serve osso buco sprinkled with gremolata. Serve with [creamy potato mash](https://www.womensweeklyfood.com.au/recipes/creamy-mashed-potatoes-9797|target="_blank") and steamed green beans.

recipes.title: Spinach, tomato and lemon one pot linguine
recipes.description: Make life easier and save washing up with this tasty vegetarian one pot pasta.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (125ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil, extra
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, extra

steps: Bring the water to the boil in a lidded large frying pan over high heat. Add the pasta, tomato, rind, oil and salt. Cover; return to the boil. As soon as it comes to the boil, uncover and cook over high heat for 8 minutes, turning the pasta regularly with a pair of tongs to stop the pasta sticking to the base of the frying pan.
Meanwhile, combine capers, olives and extra oil in a small bowl.
Remove pan from the heat. Add spinach and half the parmesan; toss gently with tongs. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Top with the caper mixture and the remaining parmesan. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: One-pot apricot chicken with creamy rice
recipes.description: A gorgeous sweet and savoury one-pot chicken meal.

ingredients.name: gram packaged french onion soup mix
ingredients.raw: 40 gram packaged french onion soup mix

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh flat-lea parsley
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh flat-lea parsley

steps: Remove and discard skin from the chicken if necessary. Heat half the oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook chicken, in batches, until browned all over; drain on paper towel.
Heat remaining oil in the same pan; cook onion, ginger, garlic and celery, stirring, for 10 minutes or until onion and celery are browned lightly.

Return chicken to pan with nectar, the water and soup mix; bring to the boil. Simmer, covered, for 10 minutes. Add rice; simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or until rice is tender. Stir in spinach. Sprinkle with parsley to serve.

recipes.title: One pot spaghetti meatballs
recipes.description: On top of spaghettiiii, all covered with cheese. I lost my poor meatball, when somebody sneezed.
steps: Thinly slice onion. Remove casings from sausages; break each sausage into three pieces to make the meatballs.

Heat oil in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker on 'sear' (HIGH) setting. Cook meatballs, turning, for 5 minutes or until browned. Transfer to a bowl. Cook onion for 5 minutes or until golden. Return meatballs to slow cooker; add passata, thyme and 2¾ cups (680ml) water. Adjust setting to HIGH; cook, covered, for 3 hours 30 minutes.

Remove lid; add spaghetti to slow cooker (break in half to fit, if necessary). Stir to cover with sauce. Cook, covered, for a further 20 minutes or until spaghetti is tender; add a little boiling water to thin sauce, if necessary. Season to taste.
Top with extra lemon thyme to serve, if you like.

recipes.title: Chicken and fennel tray bake
recipes.description: A flavoursome meal cooked in one pan meaning it's high on flavour but low on effort (and saves washing up too).

ingredients.name: garlic bulb, cut in half horizontallly
ingredients.raw: 2 garlic bulb, cut in half horizontallly

ingredients.name: sundried black olives
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (100g) sundried black olives (SEE tip)

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Thinly slice lemon; remove seeds.
Heat oil in a large flameproof roasting pan over medium heat; cook chicken for 5 minutes each side or until browned.
Add stock, lemon juice, lemon slices, rosemary, fennel, garlic and olives to pan; season well. Transfer pan to oven; roast for 1 hour or until chicken is cooked through.
Serve chicken and roasted vegetables drizzled with pan juices.

recipes.title: Curried sausage and vegetable tray bake
recipes.description: Minimal effort for maximum reward.
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Heat a large frying pan on high. Cook sausages 4-5 minutes. turning, until golden all over. Transfer to a 20 x 30cm shallow baking dish.
In the same pan, saute celery, onion and garlic 2-3 minutes. Add curry powder and chilli. Cook, stirring, 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in tomatoes, wine and water. Bring to boil. Add zucchini and eggplant. Season to taste. Pour mixture over sausages in dish.
Bake 10-15 minutes, until vegetables are tender and sausages are cooked through. Sprinkle with parsley to serve.

recipes.title: Easy one-pan roast chicken breasts with vegies
recipes.description: This one-pan wonder makes dinner and wash-up easier.

ingredients.name: grams lean ham slices, chopped finely
ingredients.raw: 20 grams lean ham slices, chopped finely

ingredients.name: trimmed, cleaned corn cob, cut into 8 equal slices
ingredients.raw: 1 (170g) trimmed, cleaned corn cob, cut into 8 equal slices

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced). Line an oven tray with baking paper.
Blend or process bread into coarse crumbs. Transfer to a bowl with ham, apricots, onion and thyme. Mix in egg to loosely bind together.
Place chicken, in a single layer, on oven tray. Brush top and sides with mustard. Press crumb mixture all over the top and sides of chicken. Bake for 10 minutes.

Toss mushrooms, tomatoes, asparagus, corn and oil in a medium bowl; season with pepper. Bake the vegetables with the chicken for a further 15 minutes or until chicken is just cooked through and crust is golden brown.
Drizzle vegetables with balsamic vinegar. Serve chicken with vegetables.

recipes.title: Tuna, spinach and broccolini baked risotto
recipes.description: Make life a little easier, well dinner at least.

ingredients.name: bacon slices, rind removed, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 100g (3 ounces) bacon slices, rind removed, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: broccolini, trimmed, thick stems halved lengthways
ingredients.raw: 175g (5½ ounces) broccolini, trimmed, thick stems halved lengthways

ingredients.name: canned tuna in oil, drained
ingredients.raw: 425g (13½ ounces) canned tuna in oil, drained

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.

Heat oil in a large heavy-based flameproof ovenproof saucepan or dish over medium heat; cook bacon, stirring, for 2 minutes or until browned. Add garlic; cook, stirring, until fragrant. Stir in rice and stock.
Cover pan with lid; transfer to oven, bake for 35 minutes. Remove pan from oven, add broccolini, cover; return to oven for 4 minutes or until broccolini is tender.
Stir in spinach, butter, tuna and half the parmesan. Season.
Combine rind and remaining parmesan; sprinkle over risotto just before serving.

recipes.title: Shredded Mexican chicken and beans
recipes.description: The delicious combination of tender shredded chicken and slow cooked beans makes this slow cooker Mexican dish absolutely delicious. Enjoy it warm for a family weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: gram canned kidney beans, rinsed, drained
ingredients.raw: 800 gram canned kidney beans, rinsed, drained

ingredients.name: medium red capsicum , chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 medium red capsicum (bell pepper) (200g), chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: medium green capsicum , chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 medium green capsicum (bell pepper) (200g), chopped coarsely

steps: Rinse chicken under cold water; pat dry, inside and out, with paper towel. Trim excess fat from chicken.

Combine beans, onion, garlic, capsicums, corn, paste, tomatoes, stock and spices in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Place chicken in cooker, push down into bean mixture. Cook, covered, on low, about 8 hours.
Remove chicken from cooker; when cool enough to handle, discard skin and bones. Shred chicken meat coarsely.
Return meat to cooker; cook, covered, on low, for 20 minutes or until hot. Season to taste.
Meanwhile, make avocado salsa: Combine ingredients in a small bowl; season to taste.
Sprinkle chicken mixture with coriander; serve with avocado salsa and tortillas. Accompany with sour cream, if you like.

recipes.title: Chicken and sage tray bake
recipes.description: This simple one-pan chicken bake borrows flavours from the Italian classic veal saltimbocca.

ingredients.name: medium brown onion, sliced into thin wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (150g) brown onion, sliced into thin wedges

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced). Place chicken, pumpkin, onion and garlic on a large, oiled, shallow baking dish. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Drizzle with olive oil; bake for 20 minutes.

Remove the tray from the oven, top the chicken with half the sage leaves and the prosciutto slices. Bake for a further 15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and the prosciutto is crisp and browned. Scatter with the remaining sage leaves. Serve with a green salad, if desired.

recipes.title: Pearl barley risotto with haloumi crumbs
recipes.description: We've swapped rice for nutty pearl barley in this beautiful pea and prosciutto recipe for a flavour and texture explosion. Try finishing your dish off with a sprinkling of haloumi crumbs to create the ultimate comfort food.

ingredients.name: gram haloumi, half grated, half crumbled
ingredients.raw: 250 gram haloumi, half grated, half crumbled

steps: In a large heavy based saucepan heat half oil on high. Cook pancetta slices 1-2 minutes each side, until crisp. Drain on paper towel.
Add half butter to pan and heat on medium until bubbling. Saute leek and garlic 4-5 minutes, until tender. Add barley and thyme and cook 2 minutes, stirring to coat grains well.
Pour in wine to deglaze pan. Simmer 1-2 minutes until amost completely evaporated.
Add stock, 1 cup at a time, stirring until almost completely absorbed between each addition and adding peas with final cup of stock (about 20 minutes).
Stir in grated haloumi, remaining butter and season. Cover and set aside 2 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a small frying pan, heat remaining oil on high. Cook crumbled haloumi 2-3 minutes, stirring, until golden and toasted. Scatter over risotto with pancetta and snow pea shoots.

recipes.title: Prawn and chorizo gumbo
recipes.description: This traditional Creole-style stew pairs juicy prawns with okra in a spiced sauce to create the ultimate American comfort food. Make it yourself at home in no time at all with this easy recipe.

ingredients.name: medium uncooked prawns, peeled and de-veined
ingredients.raw: 12 medium (600g) uncooked prawns, peeled and de-veined
1/4 cup roughly chopped flat leaf parsley

steps: Heat the oil and butter in a large saucepan, cook the onion, garlic and spices over medium heat until onion is softened and spices are fragrant. Add the capsicum, chilli, chorizo and okra and cook, stirring for a further 5 minutes.

Increase the heat and add the tomatoes and tomato paste and stir for one minute before adding the stock, bring to the boil. Add the rice and cook, covered, for 10 minutes until rice is almost tender, stirring occasionally.
Just before serving, add the prawns: simmer for 3 minutes until prawns just cooked through. Serve sprinkled with chopped parsley.

recipes.title: White wine and lamb one pot
recipes.description: Warm your body and soul with this hearty lamb and potato stew, simmered in a fragrant white wine sauce for extra flavour. It makes a brilliant family dinner dish on cooler evenings.

ingredients.name: gram boned lamb leg, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 800 gram boned lamb leg, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: gram kipfler potatoes, peeled, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 600 gram kipfler potatoes, peeled, chopped coarsely
1/3 cup coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Heat oil in a 6-litre (24-cup) pressure cooker; cook lamb, in batches, until browned. Remove from cooker.
Cook onion in cooker, stirring, until soft. Return lamb to cooker with wine; secure lid. Bring cooker to high pressure. Reduce heat to stabilise pressure; cook about 20 minutes.
Release pressure using the quick release method; remove lid. Add potato; secure lid. Bring cooker to high pressure. Reduce heat to stabilise pressure; cook about 5 minutes.
Release pressure using the quick release method; remove lid. Add peas; simmer, uncovered, until peas are tender. Remove from heat.
Whisk eggs and cheese in a medium bowl; stir into hot lamb mixture until sauce thickens. Season to taste. Serve sprinkled with parsley and extra grated parmesan cheese, if you like.

recipes.title: One pot curried chicken and rice
recipes.description: A quick and easy weeknight dinner idea.

ingredients.name: one pot curried chicken with rice
ingredients.raw: One pot curried chicken with rice

ingredients.name: 800g chicken thigh fillets, cut into pieces
ingredients.raw: 2 800g chicken thigh fillets, cut into pieces

steps: Heat a large saucepan over high heat. Add the curry paste; cook, stirring, for about 1 minute or until fragrant. Add the chicken and cook, stirring, until browned all over.
Add the rice and stock; bring to the boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 15, minutes. Remove from the heat and stir well. Cover dish, stand for 5 minutes.
Add the spinach leaves and stir to combine, season to taste with salt and pepper.

recipes.title: Beef larb
recipes.description: Take yourself on a trip to Thailand with aromatic mint, chilli, coriander and lime. This powerful beef dish is best served with a fresh, crispy lettuce leaf.

ingredients.name: thoroughly washed, roots and stems chopped
ingredients.raw: 1/2 bunch coriander, thoroughly washed, roots and stems chopped

ingredients.name: green onions , white and pale green parts only, sliced finely
ingredients.raw: 6 green onions (green shallots), white and pale green parts only, sliced finely

ingredients.name: long green chilli, seeded, finely chopped
ingredients.raw: 1 long green chilli, seeded, finely chopped (optional)

ingredients.name: baby cos lettuce, washed, leaves separated
ingredients.raw: 2 baby cos lettuce, washed, leaves separated

steps: Heat a large non-stick frying pan over medium heat. Toast rice in pan, tossing, 2-3 minutes, or until golden brown. Place in a food processor (or use a mortar and pestle) and pulse until coarsely ground.
Heat oil in the pan and brown mince, 3-4 minutes. Add garlic and coriander roots and stems. Cook, stirring, a further 2-3 minutes. Add fish sauce, lime juice and brown sugar.
Remove pan from the heat and stir through green onions and half the coriander leaves, mint leaves and chilli (if using). Sprinkle with toasted rice and remaining coriander.
Serve immediately alongside a platter of cos lettuce leaves. Serve with steamed jasmine rice and lime wedges, if desired.

recipes.title: Traditional minestrone

ingredients.name: gram can borlotti beans, drained, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 400 gram can borlotti beans, drained, rinsed

steps: In a large, heavy based saucepan, heat oil on medium. Saute onion, pancetta, garlic and rosemary for 8-10 minutes until softened, not browned.
Add potatoes, celery and carrot to pan. Saute 2-3 minutes. Stir stock and tomatoes through. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer 20 minutes, or until vegetables are almost tender.
Add risoni and both beans, simmer 10-15 minutes. Season to taste. Serve topped with shaved parmesan and chopped parsley, with crusty bread on the side.

recipes.title: Chicken, asparagus and pea casserole
recipes.description: Chicken, asparagus and pea casserole

ingredients.name: kilogram chicken thigh fillets, cut into thirds
ingredients.raw: 1.2 kilogram chicken thigh fillets, cut into thirds (halved if small fillets)

ingredients.name: asparagus, trimmed, cut into thirds
ingredients.raw: 2 bunch (340g) asparagus, trimmed, cut into thirds

steps: Heat the oil in a large heavy-based saucepan over a medium-high heat. Add the chicken; cook until browned all over. Add the carrots, onion and garlic; cook, stirring, for about 2 minutes or until starting to soften.
Add the flour; cook, stirring, until the vegetables are coated.

Gradually stir in the stock until combined, adding just enough to cover the chicken and vegetables. Simmer, uncovered, for about 15 minutes or until chicken is cooked through, topping up stock if necessary. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Add peas; return to the boil. Add asparagus; simmer, uncovered, for another few minutes or until the asparagus is just tender.
Serve chicken casserole with mashed potato, if desired.

recipes.title: Rosemary and tomato barley risotto with mozzarella
recipes.description: Mozzarella is rather delightfully known as 'fior di latte', literally meaning 'flower of the milk'. It provides a wonderfully creamy and mild balance to the spice in this rosemary and tomato barley risotto.

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup loosely packed fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves

steps: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat; cook onion, garlic, capsicum and rosemary for 5 minutes or until tender.

Add barley; cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Add stock, tomatoes, passata, sugar and rind; bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low; cook, stirring occasionally, for 45 minutes or until barley is tender. Season to taste.
Spoon risotto into bowls; top with mozzarella, drizzle with chilli oil and sprinkle with parsley.

recipes.title: Lemon-scented lamb casserole with winter vegetables
recipes.description: This lemon-scented lamb casserole is full of the flavours of roasted winter vegetables, and is delicious served with creamy mashed potatoes.

ingredients.name: gram butternut pumpkin, peeled, deseeded, cut into 2cm cubes
ingredients.raw: 800 gram butternut pumpkin, peeled, deseeded, cut into 2cm cubes

steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (140°C fan-forced). Make a bouquet garni by tying bay leaf, oregano and parsley together with kitchen string, set aside. Toss lamb in seasoned flour until well coated, shake away excess flour.
Heat 2tsp oil over medium-high heat in a large casserole dish. Cook lamb in 2 batches, adding 2tsp more oil between batches, until browned all over. Remove from dish.
Add remaining oil to dish and cook onion over medium heat until soft. Add garlic and cook, stirring, 1 minute, or until garlic is aromatic.
Add 1/4 cup (60ml) lemon juice to dish and bring to the boil. Cook, stirring, until juice is reduced by about half.
Return all the lamb with any juices to dish. Add bouquet garni, stock and parsnips, stir to combine. Bring to the boil. Cover and place in oven for 1 1/2 hours or until lamb is very tender.

Place pumpkin on top of the casserole (don't stir through). Cover and cook a further 10 minutes or until pumpkin is tender. Add remaining juice, sprinkle with rind and oregano. Serve with potatoes, if desired.

recipes.title: One-pan sausage bake

ingredients.name: medium yellow capsicums , , sliced thickly
ingredients.raw: 2 medium yellow capsicums (bell peppers), (400g), sliced thickly

ingredients.name: small red onion , cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 small red onion (100g), cut into wedges

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Heat large flameproof baking dish over high heat. Cook sausages until browned all over. Add capsicum, tomatoes and onion, season.
Roast, uncovered, about 30 minutes or until sausages are cooked through and the vegetables are tender.

recipes.title: Upside-down banana cake
steps: Preheat oven to 170°C (150°C fan-forced). Grease and line deep 20cm-round cake pan.
Stir butter and brown sugar in small saucepan over low heat until smooth. Spread mixture over base of pan; top with bananas.
Combine buttercake mix, eggs, butter and milk in medium bowl. Beat on low speed with electric mixer until ingredients are combined. Increase speed to medium; beat 3 minutes.
Pour mixture over bananas; bake about 50 minutes. Stand cake in pan 5 minutes before turning, top-side down, onto serving plate.

recipes.title: Grated potato fritters
recipes.description: Similar to a potato rosti or latke, these parcels of crispy goodness are delicious.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil for frying, or as needed
ingredients.raw: 1 cup (250ml) extra virgin olive oil for frying, or as needed

ingredients.name: avocado wedges, hot sauce, sour cream, optional, to serve
ingredients.raw: avocado wedges, hot sauce, sour cream, optional, to serve

steps: Grate the potatoes on the coarse side of a box grater. Immediately place into a large bowl of cold water as they will start to brown very quickly.

Drain the grated potatoes through a strainer and rinse until water is clear; drain well. Add grated potato to a clean tea towel and squeeze to remove any excess water (the potato should be loose and dry).
Place grated potato into a large bowl, stir in the onion, flour and egg; mix to combine. Stir in thyme or rosemary and season well with salt flakes and freshly ground pepper.

Heat enough of the olive oil to about 1.5cm deep in a large heavy-based frying pan over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, carefully drop 1/3 cup of grated potato mixture into the pan, leaving space between fritters. Shallow fry in batches, for about 5 minutes or until golden brown on the bottom. Turn and brown on the other side. It should take at least 5 minutes per side.
Remove from pan, and drain on a rack. Season with salt and pepper and serve grated potato fritters immediately with avocado, hot sauce and sour cream, if desired.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s slow cooker lamb chops
recipes.description: In a sweet and mild curry sauce.

ingredients.name: large lamb forequarter chops, excess fat trimmed
ingredients.raw: 4 (1kg) large lamb forequarter chops, excess fat trimmed

ingredients.name: large carrot, peeled, halved lengthways and cut into 1cm thick slices
ingredients.raw: 1 large (150g) carrot, peeled, halved lengthways and cut into 1cm thick slices

ingredients.name: x 400ml can chopped or crushed tomatoes
ingredients.raw: ½ x 400ml can chopped or crushed tomatoes

ingredients.name: greek yoghurt, plus extra for serving
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (140g) Greek yoghurt, plus extra for serving

ingredients.name: basmati rice, large pappadum, parsley leaves, for serving
ingredients.raw: basmati rice, large pappadum, parsley leaves, for serving

steps: In a large shallow bowl or dinner plate, combine the salt, pepper and curry powder into the flour.  Press each chop into the mixture and shake to remove excess flour.  Reserve any extra flour mixture.
In a large, oven-proof frypan over medium-high heat, add half the olive oil and brown the lamb chops.  Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the onion, carrot and garlic to the pan and saute until the onion is translucent and fragrant.
Sprinkle the reserved flour over the onion mixture and stir until combined.  Gradually add the stock, stirring, until combined. Add the tomatoes, yoghurt and brown sugar and stir to combine well.
Place the chops and sauce into the slow cooker.  Make sure the chops are submerged in liquid in the sauce; add extra beef stock if required.  Cook for 4 hours on high.
If the chops were very fatty, you may need to spoon some of the oil off the top of the pan before serving.  Serve with cooked basmati rice, pappadams and yoghurt.

recipes.title: Slow cooker beef casserole
recipes.description: The ultimate winter warmer.

ingredients.name: medium brown onions , cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 2 medium brown onions (300g), cut into wedges
1/3 cup coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Coat beef in flour; shake off excess. Heat 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large frying pan; cook beef, in batches, until browned all over. Transfer beef to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.

Heat remaining oil in same frying pan; cook onion, carrot, celery, parsnip, swede and garlic, stirring, until onion softens. Add paste; cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Remove from heat; stir in tomatoes and stock.

Stir vegetable mixture and bay leaves into cooker; add thyme. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours. Add silver beet; cook a further 10 minutes. Discard bay leaves and thyme; season to taste. Serve topped with parsley.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chicken drumsticks
recipes.description: An easy and tasty family dinner.
steps: Peel and thinly slice garlic. Peel and finely grate ginger. Add garlic, ginger, kecap manis and 2 cups (500ml) water to slow cooker. Add chicken in a single layer.

Heat slow cooker on LOW. Cook, covered, turning chicken halfway through cooking time, for 7 hours or until chicken is very tender. Transfer chicken to a large plate or tray. Strain sauce; return to cooker.
Adjust setting to ‘reduce’ (HIGH); simmer sauce for 15 minutes or until reduced slightly. Serve chicken with sauce as a glaze; season. Scatter with mint leaves.

recipes.title: Slow cooker lamb shoulder
recipes.description: Need to feed a crowd? This mango chutney lamb is great for casual entertaining.
steps: Trim lamb of any excess fat. Place ¾ cup of the chutney, the sriracha and vinegar in a small bowl; stir well.
Heat half the oil in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker on ‘sear’ (HIGH) setting. Add lamb; cook, turning, for 10 minutes or until browned all over. Season with salt and pepper.

Add sauce mixture to slow cooker; turn lamb to coat. Adjust setting to LOW; cook, covered, for 9 hours or until lamb is tender. Carefully transfer lamb to a large plate or tray. Skim excess fat from surface of sauce.
Adjust setting to ‘reduce’ (HIGH); bring sauce to the boil. Simmer for 10 minutes or until sauce is reduced and thickened. Shred lamb from bone; return meat to sauce.
Heat a chargrill pan over high heat. Brush wraps with remaining oil; grill for 1 minute on each side or until grill marks appear and wraps are warmed through. Swirl remaining chutney through yoghurt.
Serve lamb with wraps and yoghurt mixture, and extra sriracha, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow cooker massaman lamb shanks
recipes.description: Tender lamb shanks cooked low and slow until falling off the bone and served in a mild Massaman curry.

ingredients.name: gram piece pumpkin, trimmed, cut into 2cm cubes
ingredients.raw: 700 gram piece pumpkin, trimmed, cut into 2cm cubes

ingredients.name: chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup (35g) roasted unsalted peanuts, chopped coarsely

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/160°C fan-forced.
Heat half the oil in large flameproof dish; cook lamb in batches, until browned.

Heat remaining oil in same dish; cook brown onion and curry paste, stirring, 2 minutes. Add coconut milk, tamarind and stock; bring to the boil. Remove from heat, add lamb; cook in oven, covered, 2 hours. Remove lamb from dish; cover.
Add pumpkin to dish; bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, about 10 minutes or until pumpkin is tender and sauce is thickened.
Divide lamb, pumpkin and sauce among serving plates, sprinkle with nuts and green onion.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chicken curry
recipes.description: An aromatic curry rich with Malaysian-inspired flavours.

ingredients.name: cm piece fresh turmeric, peeled, chopped
fresh long red chillies, seeded, chopped
ingredients.raw: 4 fresh long red chillies, seeded, chopped

ingredients.name: cm piece galangal or ginger, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 3cm piece galangal or ginger, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: fried shallots and coriander leaves, to serve
ingredients.raw: fried shallots and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Process lemongrass, turmeric, chilli, shallots, garlic and salt in a food processor to form a paste.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook spice paste, stirring frequently, for 10 minutes or until fragrant and dry.

Transfer spice mixture to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Add chicken; toss until well coated in spice mixture. Add ½ cup (125ml) coconut milk, 1 cup (250ml) water, galangal, tamarind and lime leaves. Cook, covered, on low for 4 hours.
Stir remaining coconut milk into curry.
Serve curry topped with fried shallots and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow cooker beef stew
recipes.description: Slow-cooked until tender and full of flavour.

ingredients.name: beef chuck steak, cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 800g beef chuck steak, cut into 5cm pieces

ingredients.name: medium orange sweet potato , unpeeled, cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1 medium orange sweet potato (400g), unpeeled, cut into 5cm pieces

ingredients.name: fresh long green chilli, chopped finely
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh long green chilli, chopped finely

ingredients.name: chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 2/3 cup (160g) drained chargrilled capsicum, chopped coarsely

steps: Place beef, sweet potato, onion, tomatoes, cumin, oregano, chilli powder, fresh chilli, garlic, capsicum and stock in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours. Season to taste.
Stir in half the coriander. Serve topped with remaining coriander and a dollop of sour cream, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow cooker pea and ham soup
recipes.description: Classic comfort food at its best.

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Place ham hock, celery, carrot, onion, garlic, 2 cups of the frozen peas, the stock and 1½ cups (375ml) water in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours (or on high for 4 hours)
Remove ham hock from cooker; shred meat using two forks. Discard skin, fat and bone.
Using a stick blender, blend soup until almost smooth, leaving some chunks of carrot and a few peas unblended.
Return shredded ham to cooker with remaining peas and the zucchini. Cook, covered, on high for 20 minutes. Season to taste.
To serve, divide soup among bowls; top with parsley.

recipes.title: Slow cooker corned beef
recipes.description: Create a delicious dinner with minimal fuss.

ingredients.name: fresh flat-leaf parsley, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon fresh flat-leaf parsley, chopped coarsely

steps: Rinse beef under cold water; pat dry with absorbent paper. Place beef, onion, carrot, celery, peppercorns, vinegar and sugar in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.
Add enough of the water to barely cover beef. Cook, covered on low for 8 hours.
Remove beef from cooker; discard liquid and vegetables. Cover beef to keep warm.

Make horseradish sauce. Heat milk until hot. Melt butter in a medium saucepan, add flour; cook, stirring, 1 minute. Gradually add milk, stirring, until sauce boils and thickens. Stir in horseradish cream and parsley. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve beef with sauce.

recipes.title: Slow cooker beef ragu
recipes.description: A hearty pasta dish that elevates an economical cut of meat like gravy beef (shin) through slow cooking. Best of all, this recipe includes instructions for the slow cooker AND the oven.

ingredients.name: pappardelle with slow cooker beef ragu
ingredients.raw: Pappardelle with slow cooker beef ragu

ingredients.name: kilogram gravy beef, cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1 kilogram gravy (shin) beef, cut into 5cm pieces

ingredients.name: fresh thyme or basil, to serve
ingredients.raw: fresh thyme or basil, to serve

steps: Heat oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook onion, stirring, until softened. Add anchovy and garlic; cook about 1 minute or until fragrant. Stir in tomato paste then wine; bring to the boil. Boil, uncovered, for about 3 minutes or until reduced. Stir in undrained tomatoes and herbs.
Transfer mixture to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker with beef; mix well. Cook, covered, on low for 6-8 hours, or until beef is very tender.
Discard bay leaf. Remove beef from cooker; shred meat coarsely. Return beef to cooker and stir though sauce. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Cook pasta in a large saucepan of well-salted boiling water until just tender. Reserve 1/2 cup (125ml) of the cooking water. Drain pasta. Return pasta to pan, toss through the beef ragu and the reserved cooking water if needed.
Serve pasta with fresh thyme or basil, if desired.

recipes.title: Slow cooker lamb ragu
recipes.description: Ragù to riches! This versatile dish is perfect for entertaining.

ingredients.name: kilogram boneless lamb leg or shoulder
ingredients.raw: 1 kilogram boneless lamb leg or shoulder

ingredients.name: cornflour, mixed with 1
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon cornflour, mixed with 1/4 cup water

steps: Preheat oven to 140°C (or get a slow cooker ready).
Trim excess fat from the lamb (a small amount can remain for flavour and moisture). Cut into 4-5 big pieces. Brush all over with oil, then season with salt and pepper.
