Heat a large frying pan on high. When hot, add lamb (you may need to do this in batches) and sear until browned. Turn over to brown the other side. Set aside in a casserole dish. Add carrot, anchovies, thyme, bay leaf and peppercorns.
Tip excess fat from the lamb pan. Reduce heat to medium, then add olive oil, onions and garlic cloves, then cook, stirring, for about 5 minutes or until starting to colour.
Add red wine, stock and tomato paste, then simmer for 1 minute. Stir in cornflour mixture and spoon everything carefully over the lamb.
If baking in the oven, cover the dish and bake for 3 hours. If using a slow cooker, cover and cook on high for about 6 hours or low for 8 (or see instructions for your slow cooker).
Remove from the oven and season with salt if needed. If the meat doesn't shred easily, cook for longer.

Remove meat from dish and shred into chunks. Strain the cooking sauce through a sieve or colander into a saucepan, then simmer the strained liquid for about 5-10 minutes or until reduced to a pouring sauce. Add the meat back in.
Serve ragu over mashed potato or sweet potato, pasta or creamy polenta. Sprinkle with fresh parsley.

recipes.title: Slow cooker beef soup with chickpea & chorizo
recipes.description: A richly flavoured soup using osso bucco.

ingredients.name: beef shin pieces , on the bone
ingredients.raw: 1.2kg beef shin pieces (osso bucco), on the bone

ingredients.name: thinly sliced roasted red capsicum
ingredients.raw: ¾ cup (150g) thinly sliced roasted red capsicum

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup loosely packed fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves

steps: Heat half the oil in a large pan over medium high heat. Season beef generously. Cook for about 4 minutes each side or until well browned. Transfer to a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker.

Return pan to medium heat, add remaining oil; add leeks, cook, stirring, for about 5 minutes or until softened. Add dry sherry, cook, stirring and scraping base of pan for 1 minute. Transfer to slow cooker.
Add chickpeas, tomatoes, carrot, capsicum, garlic, bay leaves, paprika, chilli flakes, the water and stock. Cook, covered, on low, about 8 hours.
Cook chorizo in a heated small frying pan over medium heat until browned and crisp.
Ladle into heated serving bowls, top with chorizo and parsley. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Slow cooker peppered pork curry
recipes.description: Spice up dinnertime.

ingredients.name: greek yoghurt, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: 1 cup (280g) Greek yoghurt, plus extra to serve

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup loosely packed fresh coriander leaves (optional)

steps: Combine pork, onion, garlic, ginger, sugar, spices, stock, tomatoes and half the yoghurt in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours.
Discard cinnamon stick. Add spinach and remaining yoghurt to cooker. Cook, uncovered, on high for 5 minutes or until spinach wilts. Season to taste.
Serve curry topped with extra yoghurt and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow cooker spinach & ricotta lasagne
recipes.description: A vegie twist on the classic meal.
steps: Squeeze excess moisture from spinach; place spinach in large bowl. Add  ricotta, eggs and half the parmesan; mix well, season.
Spray the bowl of 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker lightly with cooking oil. Combine sauce and pesto in medium bowl, season; spread ½ cup of the  sauce mixture over base of cooker.

Place 2 lasagne sheets in cooker, breaking to fit. Spread one-third of the spinach  mixture over pasta; top with one-third of the sauce, then 2 more lasagne  sheets. Repeat layering, finishing with sauce.
Sprinkle with mozzarella and  remaining parmesan. Cook, covered, on low, 4 hours or until pasta is tender.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chicken noodle soup
recipes.description: With minimal prep, come back later to a hearty bowl – too easy!
steps: Heat oil in a large frypan over medium heat. Add onion, garlic and celery and cook for 4-6 minutes, or until softened.
Place vegetable mixture in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Add carrot, bay leaves, chicken breasts, stock and water. Season with 2 teaspoons salt flakes and 1 teaspoon cracked black pepper.
Cover with a lid and cook on high for 4 hours (or low heat for 8 hours), or until the chicken and vegetables are tender.

Remove the chicken breasts and shred into small pieces. Add spaghetti and corn, stir to combine. Cover with a lid and cook for a further 30-40 minutes, or until the pasta is tender, and soup has thickened slightly.
Return shredded chicken to the soup. Remove the bay leaves and discard. Serve with a squeeze of lemon juice and parsley.

recipes.title: Slow cooker satay pork stew
recipes.description: Comfort food made easy.

ingredients.name: pork scotch fillet , cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 700g pork scotch fillet (neck), cut into 5cm pieces

ingredients.name: medium red onion , cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 medium red onion (170g), cut into wedges

ingredients.name: fresh long red chilli, chopped finely
ingredients.raw: ½ fresh long red chilli, chopped finely

ingredients.name: coarsely chopped roasted unsalted peanuts
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (35g) coarsely chopped roasted unsalted peanuts

ingredients.name: fresh long red chilli, extra, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: ½ fresh long red chilli, extra, sliced thinly

steps: Place pork, onion, capsicum, satay sauce, soy sauce and chopped chilli in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low for 4 hours. Season to taste.
Adjust setting to ‘reduce’ (HIGH). Simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in juice and green onion. Serve stew topped with peanuts, extra sliced chilli and micro herbs.

recipes.title: How to make passata in your slow cooker
recipes.description: The beautiful base to so many dishes.
steps: Place oil in base of a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Add tomatoes to cooker; sprinkle with salt. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours, do not stir.
Carefully transfer tomatoes to a sieve with a slotted spoon; discard liquid in cooker. Blend or process tomatoes, in batches, in a high-powered blender until smooth.
Return passata to cooker, increase heat to reduce setting on high; bring to the boil.
Pour hot passata into hot sterilised bottles, seal immediately. Label and date bottles when cold.

recipes.title: Slow cooker pulled pork
recipes.description: Serve in tortillas topped with sour cream and coriander.

ingredients.name: medium red capsicums , sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 2 medium red capsicums (bell peppers) (400g), sliced thinly

ingredients.name: medium brown onions , sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 2 bunch medium brown onions (300g), sliced thinly

ingredients.name: gram bottled chunky mild tomato salsa
ingredients.raw: 375 gram bottled chunky mild tomato salsa

steps: Combine capsicum, onion, salsa, sauce, garlic, spices and oregano in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker, add pork, turn to coat in mixture. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Carefully remove pork from cooker, shred meat using two forks. Return pork to cooker, stir gently. Season to taste.
Divide pork between tortillas. Serve topped with sour cream and coriander.

recipes.title: Slow cooker sweet and sour pork
recipes.description: A family favourite dinner idea.

ingredients.name: pork scotch fillet, cut into 4cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1kg pork scotch fillet, cut into 4cm pieces

ingredients.name: chinese cooking wine
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (60ml) Chinese cooking wine (shao hsing)

ingredients.name: medium red onion , cut into thin wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 medium red onion (170g), cut into thin wedges

ingredients.name: mixed heirloom baby carrots , trimmed
ingredients.raw: 1 bunch mixed heirloom baby carrots (400g), trimmed

ingredients.name: mini red capsicums, halved, seeds removed
ingredients.raw: 350g mini red capsicums, halved, seeds removed

ingredients.name: medium pineapple , cored, cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: ½ medium pineapple (625g), cored, cut into wedges

ingredients.name: steamed jasmine rice and shredded green onion, to serve
ingredients.raw: steamed jasmine rice and shredded green onion, to serve

steps: Heat oil in a 6-litre (24-cup) slow cooker on sear (high) setting. Cook pork, in two batches, turning, for 5 minutes or until browned all over.

Combine ginger, garlic, rice wine, cooking wine, soy sauce, tomato paste and stock in a medium bowl; add to cooker with onion, carrots, capsicum and pineapple. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours or until pork is tender.
Serve pork, pineapple and vegetables with rice, drizzled with cooking liquid and topped with shredded green onion. Season to taste.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chilli con carne
recipes.description: A family favourite.

ingredients.name: coriander sprigs, mixed corn chips and lime wedges, to serve
ingredients.raw: coriander sprigs, mixed corn chips and lime wedges, to serve

steps: Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (high) setting. Cook onion and garlic, stirring, until onion softens. Add beef, cumin and chilli; cook, stirring, until browned. Stir in stock, tomato paste, tomatoes and oregano; season. Cook, covered, on low for 7½ hours.
Add beans to cooker. Cook, covered, for 1 hour. Season to taste.
Top chilli with coriander sprigs. Serve with corn chips and lime wedges.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chicken and vegetable soup
recipes.description: Comfort in a bowl.
steps: Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (high) setting. Cook onion, mushroom, celery, thyme and bay leaves, stirring, for 5 minutes or until vegetables soften. Add chicken, corn, potato and stock. Cook, covered, on low for 6 hours.
Remove chicken and corn from cooker. Shred chicken coarsely using two forks; discard skin and bones. Using a small, sharp knife, cut kernels from corn cobs; discard cobs.

Stir milk and cornflour in a small bowl until combined. Return shredded chicken and corn kernels to cooker with cornflour mixture; stir through chowder to coat. Simmer, uncovered, on high for 10 minutes or until mixture thickens. Season to taste.
Top chowder with green onion and serve with toasted sourdough.

recipes.title: Slow cooker caramel pork with crispy crackling
recipes.description: This Vietnamese-inspired pork dish pops with fresh flavours and the crunch of crisp crackling.

ingredients.name: large lebanese cucumber, julienned or shredded
ingredients.raw: 1 large Lebanese cucumber, julienned or shredded

steps: Remove the rind from the pork; place the rind on a plate. Refrigerate, uncovered, until required (see tip).
Trim and discard any excess fat from pork. Place pork in a 5.5 litre slow cooker.

Combine the brown sugar, coconut water, fish sauce and garlic in a jug. Pour evenly over pork; season. Cover with lid; cook on LOW for 6 hours, turning every 2 hours, until tender and starting to fall apart. Transfer to a board; cover with foil to keep warm.
Make the salad: Meanwhile, combine the vinegar and caster sugar in a bowl. Add the carrot and cucumber; season and toss. Set aside for at least 1 hour to pickle.
Preheat oven to 230°C (210°C fan-forced).
Place the rind on a baking paper-lined tray. Sprinkle with sea salt. Roast for 15‑20 minutes or until golden and puffed. Cut into 1cm-wide strips.
Meanwhile, transfer 1½ cups (375ml) cooking liquid to a saucepan over high heat. Bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer for 20 minutes or until reduced and thickened slightly.
Drain the pickles; toss with lettuce and coriander. Shred the pork; place on a platter. Drizzle over the sauce. Serve with crackling and salad.

recipes.title: Easy slow-cooker bolognese sauce
recipes.description: Let dinner take care of itself.

ingredients.name: ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 kg ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh basil leaves
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup finely chopped fresh Basil leaves

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh basil leaves, extra
ingredients.raw: ½ cup loosely packed fresh basil leaves, extra

steps: Heat half the oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sauté (MEDIUM) setting; cook prosciutto, stirring, until crisp. Add onion, carrot, celery and garlic; cook, stirring, until vegetables soften. Remove from cooker.

Heat remaining oil in cooker; cook pork and veal, stirring, for 5 minutes or until browned. Add wine; bring to the boil. Return vegetable mixture to cooker; stir in stock, paste and tomato. Cook, covered, on low, for 10 hours.
Stir in chopped basil and oregano; cook, covered, on high, for about 10 minutes. Season to taste.
Serve bolognese topped with parmesan and extra basil leaves

recipes.title: Slow cooker whole chicken with a peri peri marinade
recipes.description: A flavour-packed dinner idea. Dial up the heat if you like it spicy, or keep it mild for a family meal.

ingredients.raw: 2/3 cup (160ml) medium peri peri marinade (see tip)

steps: Pat chicken dry inside and out with paper towel. Cut 1 lemon into wedges. Place lemon wedges and 4 thyme sprigs inside cavity of chicken. Secure cavity with a skewer.

Make a pocket under skin of breasts, drumsticks and thighs with your fingers. Using disposable gloves, rub ¼ cup of the marinade under skin. Tuck wing tips under and tie legs together with kitchen string. Rub another 1/3 cup marinade over chicken.
Heat oil in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker on sear (high) setting. Cook chicken, turning, until browned all over.
Adjust setting to low. Cook chicken, covered, for 6 hours.
Remove chicken from cooker; cut into pieces. Cut remaining lemon into wedges. Swirl remaining peri peri marinade through mayonnaise.
Season chicken. Serve topped with remaining thyme sprigs, and with mayonnaise mixture and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Slow cooker satay chicken
recipes.description: A flavour-packed dinner idea.

ingredients.name: cm piece fresh ginger, grated finely
fresh small red chilli, seeded, chopped
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh small red chilli, seeded, chopped

ingredients.name: natural crunchy dark roast peanut butter
ingredients.raw: 1 cup (280g) natural crunchy dark roast peanut butter

ingredients.name: cooked rice, extra sliced chilli, lime wedges and coriander leaves, to serve
ingredients.raw: cooked rice, extra sliced chilli, lime wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Process oil, lemongrass, onion, ginger, garlic, chopped chilli and 2 lime leaves in a small food processor until as smooth as possible. Spoon mixture into a bowl. Add palm sugar, tamarind, peanut butter and soy sauce; mix to a paste.
Place chicken, in a single layer, in the base of a greased 7-litre (28-cup) slow cooker. Spread chicken with the peanut paste. Add stock and remaining lime leaves.
Cook, covered, on low for 3½ hours or until chicken is tender.
Preheat oven grill.
Transfer chicken to a greased foil-covered oven tray. Cook under the grill for 3 minutes or until starting to brown. Stir lime juice into sauce in cooker. Season to taste.
Serve chicken drizzled with sauce, topped with extra sliced chilli, lime wedges and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker massaman beef curry
recipes.description: This Thai massaman curry is slow-cooked, meltingly-tender, and perfectly rich, topped with crunchy peanuts.

ingredients.name: brown onions , cut into thin wedges
ingredients.raw: 2 brown onions (400g), cut into thin wedges
1/3 cup lightly packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Heat half the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat; cook onion, stirring, for 10 minutes or until browned lightly. Transfer mixture to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.
Heat remaining oil in same pan over high heat; cook beef, in batches, until browned. Add paste; cook, stirring, 1 minute or until fragrant. Transfer mixture to cooker.
Add coconut milk, stock, cinnamon, bay leaves, potato and nuts to cooker. Cook, covered, on low, 8 hours.
Stir in sugar and sauce. Serve curry topped with coriander; accompany with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chicken pot pie soup
recipes.description: When two favourites combine.

ingredients.name: medium leek, trimmed and sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (300g) leek, trimmed and sliced thinly

ingredients.name: green beans, trimmed and cut into 3cm lengths
ingredients.raw: 200g green beans, trimmed and cut into 3cm lengths

steps: Heat the oil in the slow cooker on the searing setting. Dust the chicken in seasoned flour, cook the chicken, in batches, for about 5 minutes, turning or until golden. Remove from slow cooker and set aside.

Add the carrot, celery and leek; cook, stirring, until starting to soften. Add the wine, mustard, bay and thyme; stir to combine.  Add the hot stock and return the chicken to the slow cooker. Cover with the lid and cook on high for 2 ½ hours, or until the chicken is very tender.
Remove the thyme and bay laves and discard. Using two forks, break up the chicken into smaller pieces, Add the green beans, peas and cream. Cook, uncovered for 30 minutes or until beans are tender.

Meanwhile, preheat oven to 220C (200C fan forced). Using an 8cm round cutter, cut 12 circles from each pastry sheet. Press two circles on top of each other. Using a fork crimp the edges. Place on a large oven tray lined with non-stick baking paper. Brush with egg, sprinkle with pepper. Bake for 15 minutes, or until golden and puffed.
To serve, divide soup between warmed bowls, sprinkle with parsley and serve with puff pastry croutes.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker chilli and fennel pork gyros
recipes.description: A delicious lunch idea.

ingredients.name: kilogram piece pork shoulder on the bone, rind on
ingredients.raw: 1 kilogram piece pork shoulder on the bone, rind on

ingredients.name: fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves, to serve
ingredients.raw: fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves, to serve

steps: Using a sharp knife, score the pork rind in a criss-cross pattern. Coarsely grate rind from lemon, then chop the lemon coarsely.
Cook fennel seeds in dry large frying pan until fragrant. Using mortar and pestle, crush seeds. Add chilli, salt, pepper, garlic, lemon rind and 2 tablespoons of the oil; pound until ground finely.
Heat remaining oil in same pan; cook pork, skin-side down, until browned and crisp. Turn pork; cook until browned all over. Spread fennel mixture all over pork.

Place onion, stock and chopped lemon in 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; top with pork, skin-side up. Cook, covered, on low, for 7 hours. Remove pork from cooker; stand, covered, for 10 minutes before shredding into pieces.
Top bread with pork and Greek salad; drizzle with tzatziki and sprinkle with parsley. Fold bread to enclose filling; serve immediately.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker fish curry
recipes.description: A fragrant Caribbean-inspired curry.

ingredients.name: freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.raw: ½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.name: finely chopped coriander roots and stems
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup finely chopped coriander roots and stems

ingredients.name: medium red capsicums , cut into 3cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 2 medium red capsicums (400g), cut into 3cm pieces

ingredients.name: thyme sprigs, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: 5 thyme sprigs, plus extra to serve

steps: Heat a small frying pan over high heat. Cook ground coriander, cumin, turmeric, ground ginger, onion powder, pepper and cloves, stirring, for 1 minute until fragrant. Remove spice mix from pan; reserve 2 teaspoons.

Heat oil in a 6-litre (24-cup) slow cooker on sear (high) setting. Cook green onion, garlic, ginger, coriander roots and stems, and capsicum for 5 minutes, stirring, or until capsicum is lightly golden.
Add spice mix, thyme, coconut milk, stock and potatoes to cooker; stir to combine. Cook, covered, on low for 4 hours or until potatoes are tender.
Add fish to cooker. Cook, covered, for 10 minutes or until fish is just cooked through. Stir in lime juice.
To serve, divide fish, potatoes, capsicum and sauce between bowls. Top curry with yoghurt and sprinkle with reserved spice mix and extra thyme. Season to taste.

recipes.title: Slow cooker meatballs
recipes.description: A set-and-forget recipe for busy days and lazy nights.

ingredients.name: medium brown onion , extra, chopped finely
ingredients.raw: 1 medium brown onion (150g), extra, chopped finely

ingredients.name: loosely packed basil leaves
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup loosely packed basil leaves

steps: Combine bread and milk in a large bowl; stand for 10 minutes. Add beef, onion, carrot, garlic, egg, tomato paste, oregano and chopped basil; season, mix well. Shape level tablespoons of mixture into balls. Place in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.

Heat oil in a large frying pan; cook extra onion and garlic, stirring, until onion softens. Stir in tomatoes, extra paste and stock; transfer to cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for about 6 hours. Season to taste.
Serve sprinkled with basil leaves.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker beef stroganoff
recipes.description: Destined to be a family favourite on high rotation in the cooler months.

ingredients.name: sweet paprika, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon sweet paprika, plus extra to serve

ingredients.name: crème fraîche, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup (80g) crème fraîche, plus extra to serve

steps: Trim beef to remove any excess fat; cut into 4cm pieces.
Heat the oil in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting. Cook beef, in batches, turning, for 5 minutes or until well browned. Remove from cooker.

Add shallots, mushrooms (halve some mushrooms, if you like) and paprika to slow cooker; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes or until fragrant. Return beef to cooker. Add stock and thyme. Adjust setting to LOW; cook, covered, for 9 hours 30 minutes or until beef is very tender.
Stir in crème fraîche; cook for a further 1 minute or until heated through. Season to taste.
Serve stroganoff drizzled with extra crème fraîche and sprinkled with extra paprika.

recipes.title: Slow cooker Greek lamb stifado
recipes.description: If you're in the mood for comfort food, then try this hearty, Greek stew.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1 kg lamb shoulder, cut into 5cm pieces

steps: Toss lamb in flour to coast, shake off excess; place in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Sprinkle lamb evenly with any excess flour.

Peel onions, leaving root ends intact. Add onions, garlic, herbs and spices to cooker. Pour over combined vinegar, paste, wine and stock. Cook, covered, on low, about 6 hours. Discard bay leaves, rosemary, Greek oregano, cinnamon and cloves. Season to taste.
Serve with fetta and extra Greek oregano, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow cooker chickpea and sweet potato curry
recipes.description: This vegan curry recipe is perfect for chilly nights. It's an easy chickpea curry made with sweet potato and a korma curry paste.

ingredients.name: gluten-free korma curry paste
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup (100g) gluten-free korma curry paste

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh curry leaves
ingredients.raw: ½ cup loosely packed fresh curry leaves

ingredients.name: grams orange sweet potato, unpeeled, sliced thickly
ingredients.raw: 500 grams orange sweet potato, unpeeled, sliced thickly

steps: Combine coconut cream, curry paste, onion and curry leaves in a large bowl. Add both the sweet potatoes and the chickpeas, season and toss to coat.

Layer mixture in a 5.5-litre (22-cup) slow cooker. Scrape any remaining sauce from the bowl and spread over the top, pressing lightly to compress the sweet potatoes. Cook, covered, on LOW for 6½ hours or HIGH for 3¼ hours, or until potatoes are tender.
To serve, divide curry among plates; scatter with coriander.

recipes.title: Slow cooker Hainanese-style chicken
recipes.description: The slow cooker is perfect for cooking Hainanese chicken rice.

ingredients.name: coriander, stems chopped coarsely, leaves picked
ingredients.raw: 1 bunch coriander, stems chopped coarsely, leaves picked

ingredients.name: tbspp sesame oil, plus extra to drizzle
ingredients.raw: 1 tbspp sesame oil, plus extra to drizzle

ingredients.name: chicken marylands or 8 small chicken thigh cutlets
ingredients.raw: 4 chicken Marylands (1.4kg) or 8 small chicken thigh cutlets

ingredients.name: lebanese cucumber , cut in half lengthways, sliced
ingredients.raw: 1 Lebanese cucumber (130g) , cut in half lengthways, sliced

steps: Place green onion, coriander stems, chilli, ginger, garlic, 2 teaspoons of the sesame oil, the sugar and salt in a tall jug. Using a stick blender, blend until smooth.

Place chicken in a 6.5-litre (26-cup) slow cooker and rub with half the green onion paste to coat all over; reserve remaining green onion paste. Add soy sauce, Shaoxing wine and 3 cups (750ml) water to cooker; stir to combine. Cook, covered, on LOW 
for 3 hours or HIGH for 2 hours.
Stir in lime juice, 1 tablespoon water and remaining sesame oil to reserved green onion paste. Add sugar to taste if needed.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully transfer chicken to a shallow platter. Strain cooking broth through a large sieve lined with paper towel into a large jug.
Serve chicken with green onion sauce, coriander leaves, cucumber, steamed Asian greens, coconut rice, lime wedges and broth on the side.

recipes.title: Slow cooker biryani
recipes.description: This biryani recipes uses brown rice and beef, and is made in the slow cooker.

ingredients.name: beef chuck steak, trimmed, cut into 4cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1 kg beef chuck steak, trimmed, cut into 4cm pieces

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup (60ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: coriander, leaves picked, steams and roots chopped finely
ingredients.raw: 1 bunch coriander, leaves picked, steams and roots chopped finely

ingredients.name: medium red onion, sliced thinly into rounds
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (170g) red onion, sliced thinly into rounds

steps: Season the beef. Heat 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook beef, in batches, for 4 minutes or until lightly golden all over. Transfer to a large bowl.

Heat remaining oil in the same pan. Cook chopped onion, garlic, ginger, carrot and coriander stems and roots, stirring, for 5 minutes or until softened. Add the curry paste; cook, stirring, for  2 minutes or until fragrant. Add 2 cups (500ml) water; stir to release any bits caught on the base of the pan.
Transfer curry sauce to a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Add beef and season. Cook, covered, on LOW for 4½ hours or HIGH for 2½ hours.
Stir in rice and 2 cups (500ml) water to cooker. Cook, covered, for a further 2½ hours on LOW or 1¾ hours on HIGH, or until rice is tender and almost all the liquid is absorbed.
Meanwhile, to make the pickled red onion, combine vinegar and the salt flakes in a medium bowl; add sliced red onion. Stand for 2 hours. Drain before using.
To serve, divide beef biryani among plates or bowls; top with a spoonful of yoghurt, drained pickled red onion, chopped cashews and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow cooker pork and fennel meatball pasta
recipes.description: The pork and fennel meatballs are served atop a blend of spaghetti and zoodles, making this a lower carb option.

ingredients.name: tbsps extra virgin olive oil, plus extra to drizzle
ingredients.raw: 2 tbsps extra virgin olive oil, plus extra to drizzle

steps: Heat half the oil in a small frying pan over low-medium heat; cook eschalot and garlic for 5 minutes or until eschalot is soft. Transfer to a medium bowl and cool for 10 minutes.
Add pork, fennel seeds, parsley, breadcrumbs and egg to bowl with eschalot and garlic, then season; mix until well combined. Shape 2 tablespoons of pork mixture to make 16 meatballs.
Heat remaining oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook meatballs in batches, turning, for 10 minutes or until browned. Transfer to a plate.

Add verjuice to pan; stir to release any bits caught on the base of the pan. Transfer liquid to a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Add bay leaf, parmesan rind, 2 cups (500ml) water and meatballs. Cook, covered, on LOW for 4 hours or HIGH for 2 hours.
Cook spaghetti following packet directions; reserve ½ cup (125ml) pasta water, then drain pasta.
Remove the bay leaf and parmesan rind from the cooker and discard. Add spaghetti and zoodles; toss gently to coat. Add a little reserved pasta water if needed. Season.

To serve, divide spaghetti, zoodles and meatballs among bowls; ladle over broth. Scatter with mint leaves and sprinkle with cracked pepper and grated parmesan. Finish with a drizzle of extra olive oil.

recipes.title: Slow cooker lamb stew
recipes.description: A delicious slow cooker recipe.

ingredients.name: kilogram lamb neck or forequarter chops
ingredients.raw: 1.2 kilogram lamb neck or forequarter chops

steps: Toss lamb in flour to coat; shake off excess. Heat half the oil in a large frying pan. In batches, stir lamb over medium-high heat until browned. Transfer to a 4.5-litre slow cooker.

Add wine to the same pan; bring to the boil. Boil, stirring occasionally, for about 5 minutes or until liquid is reduced by half. Transfer to slow cooker. Add onion, potato, carrot, paste, rosemary and stock. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Divide lamb and vegetables among plates. Spoon over a little cooking liquid to serve.

recipes.title: Slow cooker curried lentil and tomato soup
recipes.description: A delicious soup made in the slow cooker.

ingredients.name: grams packet lentil soup mix, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 375 grams packet lentil soup mix, rinsed

ingredients.name: coriander, stems chopped finely, leaves picked
ingredients.raw: 1 bunch coriander, stems chopped finely, leaves picked

steps: Place soup mix, tomatoes, stock, curry powder, oil, onion, garlic and coriander stems in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker; stir to combine. Cook, covered, on LOW for 8 hours or HIGH for 4 hours, or until lentils are tender.

To serve, divide soup among bowls; drizzle with coconut cream and scatter with toasted coconut flakes and coriander leaves. 
*Suitable to freeze.*

recipes.title: Slow cooker beef ramen with shiitake mushrooms
recipes.description: A fabulous slow cooked version of a Japanese classic.

ingredients.name: chuck steak, cut into 5cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 1 kg chuck steak, cut into 5cm pieces

ingredients.name: coriander, stems chopped, leaves picked
ingredients.raw: ½ bunch coriander, stems chopped, leaves picked

ingredients.name: watermelon radishes, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: ½ bunch watermelon radishes, sliced thinly

steps: To make the spice pouch, combine ingredients in a square of muslin cloth; pull corners together and tie with string to secure.
Heat sesame oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook chuck steak in batches for 4-5 minutes or until browned.

Place the spice pouch, garlic, stock, soy sauce, onion, shiitake mushrooms and ½ cup (125ml) water in a 3.7-litre (15-cup) slow cooker; stir to combine. Add the chuck steak. Cook, covered, on LOW for 4 hours or HIGH for 2½ hours, or until chuck steak can be shredded with a fork.

Remove the spice pouch from the cooker and discard. Using a slotted spoon, transfer chuck steak to a medium bowl. Using two forks, coarsely shred meat. Stir shredded chuck steak into the cooking liquid. Stir in chopped coriander stems and the juice from 1 lime. Cut the remaining lime into wedges.
Cook noodles following packet directions. Add snow peas to noodles for the last minute of cooking time; drain.
To serve, divide noodles and snow peas among bowls; ladle over broth, chuck steak and shiitake mushrooms. Top with sliced radishes and coriander leaves. Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Slow cooker butter chicken
recipes.description: The cream is swapped for raw cashews in this twist on butter chicken.

ingredients.name: medium orange sweet potato, cut into 2cm rounds
ingredients.raw: 1 medium (400g) orange sweet potato, cut into 2cm rounds

steps: Place cashews in a medium bowl and cover with cold water; stand for 30 minutes or until plump and pale in colour.
Meanwhile, place chicken, buttermilk and spices in a large bowl; stir well to coat chicken completely. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Drain chicken; reserve marinade. Heat oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Cook chicken in batches for 3 minutes each side or until lightly golden. Transfer chicken to a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Wipe the pan clean.

Place onion in the same pan; cook, stirring, for 4 minutes or until starting to soften. Stir in tomato paste and reserved marinade. Cook for 2 minutes or until mixture comes to a simmer and is fragrant.

Transfer onion mixture to the cooker. Stir in sweet potato, carrot and stock. Cook, covered, on LOW for 4½ hours or HIGH for 2½ hours, or until chicken is falling off the bone and vegetables are tender.

Meanwhile, drain cashews; reserve ½ cup of the soaking liquid. Process cashews and reserved soaking liquid to a smooth paste (use a high-powered blender if you have one, as this will achieve a very smooth result). Set aside until needed.

Transfer ½ cup (125ml) of the curry sauce to a large jug; stir in cashew paste until smooth. Return curry sauce to cooker and stir to combine. Add the corn. Cook, uncovered, for a further 30 minutes on HIGH or until sauce thickens and coats the back of a wooden spoon. Stir in spinach until just beginning to wilt. Season.
To serve, divide butter chicken among bowls; scatter with chopped cashews. Serve with steamed basmati rice.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker coconut pumpkin soup
recipes.description: Coconut milk, red chilli and coriander give this slow cooker pumpkin soup recipe a flavour boost.

ingredients.name: fresh long red chilli, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh long red chilli, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: loosely packed small fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup loosely packed small fresh coriander leaves

steps: Place garlic, dried chilli, pumpkin, onion, coconut milk and stock in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on high, for 4 hours.
Cool soup for 10 minutes. Using a stick blender blend soup until smooth. Season to taste.
Serve soup topped with peanuts, fresh chilli and coriander.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker beer and thyme beef cheeks
recipes.description: A great dinner party main for the colder months.
steps: Peel onions, leaving root end intact. Combine onions with celery, carrots, thyme, beer, stock, paste, sauce, sugar and mustard in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Add beef; turn to coat in mixture. Cook, covered, on low, about 9½ hours.
Discard thyme. Add beans to cooker; cook, covered, on low, for 30 minutes. Season to taste.

Serve beef sprinkled with extra fresh thyme, if you like.
Serving suggestion:  Creamy mashed potato or cheesy polenta.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker beef cheeks in pedro ximénez
recipes.description: A delicious dinner party main perfect for the cooler months.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.raw: ⅓ cup (80ml) extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: fresh thyme sprigs, plus extra to serve
ingredients.raw: 8 fresh thyme sprigs, plus extra to serve

steps:
Season flour with salt and pepper. Toss beef in flour to coat. 
Heat half the oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook beef, on both sides, until browned. Transfer to a 6-litre (24-cup) slow cooker.

Heat remaining oil in same pan. Cook onion and carrot for 5 minutes; add to cooker with garlic, pedro ximénez, wine, stock, bay leaves and thyme. Cover mixture directly with baking paper. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours (or on high for 4½ hours). Remove bay leaves and thyme sprigs.
Serve beef cheeks topped with extra thyme.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker spicy upside down pineapple chicken
recipes.description: A delicious way to cook a whole chook in the slow-cooker.

ingredients.name: lemongrass, white part only, chopped finely
ingredients.raw:
1 stalk lemongrass, white part only, 
chopped finely

ingredients.name: chopped coriander roots and stems
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup chopped coriander roots and stems

steps: Peel and trim pineapple. Cut pineapple into 1cm rounds; remove core.
Process onion, garlic, ginger, chilli, lemongrass, coriander roots and stems, ground coriander, lime juice and soy sauce in a food processor until chopped finely.
Transfer onion mixture to a large bowl, add chicken; turn to coat chicken completely.

Place pineapple slices, slightly overlapping, over the base of a 6-litre (24-cup) slow cooker. Place chicken, breast-side down, and onion mixture on pineapple. Cook, covered, on low for 4 hours or until chicken is tender.
Line a baking tray with foil. Carefully transfer chicken, skin-side up, then pineapple to tray.
Add rice to cooker; stir to combine. Cook, covered, on low for 35 minutes; season. Turn off cooker; stand for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, preheat grill to high. Lightly spray chicken and pineapple with oil. Place under grill for 10 minutes or until chicken is golden brown.
Serve chicken with pineapple, rice and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker cashew and cauliflower curry
recipes.description: A fragrant vegan curry that's packed with flavour.
steps: Preheat a 6-litre (24-cup) slow cooker on high.
Melt coconut oil in the cooker. Add onion, garlic, ginger and garam masala; cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly fragrant. Stir in tomato paste.
Add cauliflower and cashews; stir until well coated in mixture. Add canned tomatoes, coconut cream and stock; season well with salt and pepper.

Turn the cooker to low; cook, covered, for 5 hours or until
cauliflower and cashews are tender.
Top curry with coriander; serve with roti.

recipes.title: Deep-pan pepperoni pizza
recipes.description: Who knew you could make pizza in the slow-cooker? We did! Here's our easier-than-ever spin using store-bought pizza dough and sauce.

ingredients.name: thick pasta sauce
ingredients.raw: ½ cup (130g) thick pasta sauce (see tip)

steps: Preheat slow cooker on LOW.

Remove slow cooker insert from base and line with foil; spray foil well with oil. Remove dough from packets and knead together lightly to form a ball. Place dough in insert. Return insert to slow cooker. Cook, covered, for 45 minutes to prove.

Adjust setting to HIGH. Push dough down lightly, pressing it out towards the side of insert to cover the base and create a thick rim. Place half the pepperoni over base. Spread over pasta sauce, then top with remaining pepperoni and the cheese. Cook, covered, for 4 hours.
Preheat oven grill on medium.
Remove pizza from slow cooker; place on an oven tray. Grill for 10 minutes or until cheese and crust are browned.
Top pizza with basil; serve straight away.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb madras
recipes.description: Put your slow cooker to good use with this rich slow-cooked curry recipe.

ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup loosely packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting; cook lamb, stirring, for 15 minutes or until well browned. Add onion, garlic, ginger and cinnamon. Cook mixture, stirring for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Add curry paste and stock; stir to combine. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours. Season to taste.
Serve sprinkled with almonds and coriander leaves.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker chicken tikka masala
recipes.description: This slow cooker chicken tikka masala recipe creates a richly flavoured curry with tender chicken. Best of all, you barely need to lift a finger!

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.raw: 1 cup loosely packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Combine chicken, tomatoes, onion and paste in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; cook, covered, on high, for 4 hours. Season to taste.
Serve drizzled with cream, topped with coriander.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb shanks
recipes.description: A classic winter warmer.
steps: Heat half the oil in a large frying pan. Toss lamb in flour that has been seasoned with salt and pepper; shake away excess. Cook lamb over high heat until browned all over; transfer to the bowl of a slow cooker.

Add remaining oil to the same pan with onions, carrots and garlic; cook, stirring, over medium heat for about 2 minutes or until the onion is starting to soften. Add the tomato paste; cook, stirring, for a further minute.

Add the wine to the pan; bring to the boil. Stir in stock, undrained tomatoes, sugar and bouquet garni. Bring to the boil; pour over the lamb in the slow cooker, making sure the lamb is covered by the liquid. Cover; cook for 6-8 hours on the low setting or until lamb is soft and falling off the bone.

Remove the lamb from the slow cooker; cover to keep warm. Turn the cooker to the high setting; cook, uncovered, for about 30 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly. Remove any fat from the surface. Serve the lamb with the sauce, mashed potatoes and steamed green vegetables, if desired.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker five-ingredient berry cobbler
recipes.description: Only five ingredients and staples needed to create this simple dessert in your slow cooker.
steps: Preheat slow cooker on HIGH.

Remove rind from orange in long, thin strips using a zesting tool; refrigerate until serving. Juice the orange. Place slightly thawed berries, cornflour and ¼ cup of the icing sugar in slow cooker; mix well. Pour over orange juice; cook, covered, for 35 minutes.

Meanwhile, mix scone mix and 1 cup (250ml) water together until combined. Turn dough onto a work surface dusted lightly with extra cornflour. Using cornflour-dusted hands, flatten dough to 3cm (1¼in) thick. Cut into 4cm (1½in) rounds using a scone cutter. Re-roll scraps and repeat. Place dough rounds on top of berry mixture. Cover slow cooker insert with a clean tea towel, then replace cooker lid. Cook, covered, for a further 1 hour or until scone topping is cooked through.
Dust with remaining icing sugar; top with orange rind.

recipes.title: Slow cooker honey soy lamb chops
recipes.description: Set and forget to create these easy lamb chops in your slow cooker.
steps: Trim chops to remove excess fat. Cut 2 onions into thick wedges.
Combine soy sauce, honey and 1 tablespoon of the oil in a small jug.
Heat a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker on LOW. Place onion wedges in cooker; top with lamb and soy sauce mixture. Cook, covered, for 6 hours.

Remove lamb and onion from slow cooker; cover to keep warm. Skim fat from liquid. Adjust setting to 'reduce' (HIGH); cook liquid, uncovered, for 15 minutes or until slightly sticky. Strain sauce through a fine sieve into a medium jug.
To make salad, combine remaining oil and vinegar in a large bowl. Thinly slice remaining red onion. Add onion and kale to bowl with dressing; season and toss gently.
Serve lamb and onion with sauce and salad.

recipes.title: Slow cooker five ingredient pecan pie
recipes.description: The classic dessert made easier using your slow cooker and five simple ingredients.
steps: Remove pastry sheet from freezer to thaw.

Preheat slow cooker on HIGH. Grease a 20cm loose-based tart tin with a little extra butter. Line tin with pastry; trim excess pastry, leaving about 1cm overhang. Prick pastry with a fork; freeze while preparing the filling.

Melt butter in a microwave or small saucepan over medium heat. Whisk syrup, eggs, flour and melted butter in a jug; stir in pecans. Pour pecan mixture into pastry case, spreading filling evenly. Place tin in slow cooker. Cover slow cooker insert with a clean tea towel, then replace cooker lid. Cook, covered, for 1 hour 45 minutes.
Remove pie from slow cooker; cool slightly. Serve pie drizzled with extra golden syrup, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow cooker five ingredient cottage pie

ingredients.name: grams jar tomato, onion & roast garlic pasta sauce
ingredients.raw: 500 grams jar tomato, onion & roast garlic pasta sauce

steps: Heat oil in a rectangular slow cooker on HIGH.

Finely chop onion; cook for 5 minutes or until softened. Add half the beef, season; cook, uncovered, stirring, for 10 minutes or until well browned. Transfer to a bowl; repeat with the remaining beef. Return all beef mince to slow cooker.
Adjust setting to LOW. Add pasta sauce and vegetables to slow cooker; stir to combine. Cook, covered, for 5 hours or until sauce is reduced.
Spoon mashed potato over beef mixture.
Cover slow cooker insert with a clean tea towel, then replace cooker lid; cook, covered, for a further 1 hour 15 minutes.
Stand for 10 minutes. Season; serve.

recipes.title: Slow cooker Greek ‘sausages’ with olives and risoni
recipes.description: A tasty slow-cooked homemade sausages cooked in a garlicky tomato sauce.

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh oregano, plus extra leaves to serve
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh oregano, plus extra leaves to serve

steps: Coarsely grate half an onion. Thinly slice the remaining 1½ onions.

Make 'sausages'. Combine lamb, grated onion (from step 1), 125g (4oz) of the fetta, the egg, breadcrumbs, spices, chopped oregano, currants and salt, using your hands to mix very well. Season with pepper. Shape ¼ cups of mixture into oval sausage shapes; place on a tray. Cover; refrigerate until required.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat; cook 'sausages', turning, for 2 minutes or until browned. Transfer to a tray.

Add sliced onion to same pan; cook, stirring, for 5 minutes or until soft and golden. Add garlic; cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant. Add canned tomatoes, paste, stock, sugar and vinegar, then season well with salt and pepper; bring to the boil. Transfer mixture to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker along with the 'sausages'.
Cook, covered, on low, for 3 hours (or high for 1½ hours). Add risoni and olives; stir gently to combine. Cook, covered, for a further 20 minutes or until risoni is tender.
Serve topped with remaining crumbled fetta and extra oregano leaves.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker pumpkin soup
recipes.description: Slow-cooked for ease and extra flavour.
steps: Heat butter and oil in large frying pan; cook leek, stirring, until soft.
Combine leek, pumpkin, potato, stock and the water in 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, 6 hours.
Cool soup 10 minutes. Blend or process soup, in batches, until smooth. Return soup to cooker. Cook, covered, on high, about 20 minutes or until hot. Stir in 1/3 cup of the cream. Season to taste.
Serve soup topped with remaining cream and chives.

recipes.title: Slow cooker lamb keema
recipes.description: This curry is not made in a hurry.

ingredients.name: fresh long green chillies, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 2 fresh long green chillies, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh coriander
ingredients.raw: ½ cup finely chopped fresh coriander

ingredients.name: thinly sliced preserved lemon rind
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons thinly sliced preserved lemon rind

steps: Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting; cook onion and garlic, stirring, for 5 minutes or until onion softens. Add lamb; cook, stirring, for 5 minutes or until browned.
Add ginger, chilli and spices; cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant. Add flour; cook, stirring for 1 minute. Add tomatoes. Cook, covered, on low, for 4 hours. Season to taste.
Increase heat to reduce setting on high; simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes or until sauce is thick.
Stir in peas, juice and half the fresh coriander. Serve topped with yoghurt, remaining coriander, almonds and preserved lemon.

recipes.title: Slow cooker lamb shanks with tomato, chilli and fetta
recipes.description: Shank you very much!

ingredients.name: small fennel bulbs , sliced thinly, fronds reserved
ingredients.raw: 2 small fennel bulbs (400g), sliced thinly, fronds reserved

ingredients.name: fresh long red chilli, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh long red chilli, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh-flat leaf parsley
ingredients.raw: ¼ cup finely chopped fresh-flat leaf parsley

steps: Place flour in a large bowl; season.

Heat half the oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting. Coat lamb in flour; shake off excess. Cook lamb, turning, in batches, for 15 minutes, or until well browned. Remove from cooker.
Heat remaining oil in cooker on sauté (MEDIUM) setting; cook onion, fennel, carrot, garlic and half the chilli, stirring, for 5 minutes or until vegetables soften.
Add wine and tomato puree; stir to combine. Return lamb to slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours. Season to taste.
Serve lamb shanks sprinkled with fetta, parsley, remaining chilli and reserved fronds.

recipes.title: Slow cooker veal rolls (involtini) with prosciutto and sage
recipes.description: Prep early then cook it low and slow.

ingredients.name: drained chargrilled capsicum strips
ingredients.raw: ⅔ cup (130g) drained chargrilled capsicum strips

steps: Place prosciutto slices on a chopping board; top each slice with a schnitzel. Place capsicum across centre of schnitzels. Top each with a sage leaf. Roll to enclose filling; secure veal rolls with toothpicks or kitchen string.

Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting; cook veal rolls, turning, for 10 minutes or until well browned. Add onion, garlic, wine and sauce. Cook, covered, on low, for 6 hours. Season to taste.
Serve veal rolls with sauce.

recipes.title: Slow cooker bean bourgignon
recipes.description: Hearty vegetarian comfort food.

ingredients.name: grams swiss brown mushrooms, halved if large
ingredients.raw: 600 grams swiss brown mushrooms, halved if large

ingredients.name: x 400g canned butter beans, drained, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 3 x 400g canned butter beans, drained, rinsed

ingredients.name: fresh thyme, plus extra small sprigs to serve
ingredients.raw: 6 sprigs fresh thyme, plus extra small sprigs to serve

steps: Place mushrooms, beans, shallots, garlic, bay leaves, thyme, wine and puree in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on high, for 4 hours.
Discard bay leaves and thyme; season to taste. Serve bourguignon topped with extra thyme.

recipes.title: Slow cooker pineapple coconut cake
recipes.description: Your slow cooker is not just for stews and lamb shanks!
steps: Chop butter into cubes; leave at room temperature to soften.
Preheat a 5.5-litre (22-cup) slow cooker on HIGH.

Grease a deep 20cm (8in) round cake pan; line base and side
with baking paper.

Drain pineapple, reserving ⅔ cup of the syrup. Pat seven pineapple slices dry with paper towel; arrange over base of lined pan. Without shaking coconut cream can, open can and spoon off 1 cup of the thickest coconut cream into a jug; refrigerate until serving. Reserve ½ cup of remaining coconut cream.

Beat softened butter and ¾ cup of the sugar in a bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Add 1 egg at a time, beating well after each addition. Fold through sifted flour and the reserved ½ cup coconut cream until just combined.

Spoon batter into pan, tapping on the bench to remove air bubbles. Smooth surface with the back of the spoon. Cut a 1 metre (39in) long piece of foil. Wrap foil around outside of pan, joining ends together on top to make a 'handle' so it looks like a basket, to assist with removing the pan from the slow cooker. Place pan inside slow cooker. Cover slow cooker insert with a clean tea towel, then replace cooker lid. Cook for 6 hours or until a skewer inserted into the centre of the cake comes out clean. Using the foil handle, remove the pan from slow cooker; cool cake in pan for 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, to make syrup, place remaining sugar and reserved pineapple syrup in the clean slow cooker insert. Adjust setting
to 'sear' (HIGH). Stir until sugar is dissolved. Bring to the boil; simmer for 10 minutes or until syrupy. (Or use a small saucepan
on the stovetop.)

Run a flat-bladed knife around edge of pan; carefully turn cake onto a plate. Just before serving, whisk refrigerated thick coconut cream in a small bowl with an electric mixer until thick. Drizzle syrup over cake; serve with whipped coconut cream.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker silverbeet dhal
recipes.description: Set and forget.

ingredients.name: fresh long green chilli, chopped finely
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh long green chilli, chopped finely

ingredients.name: medium silverbeet leaves , stems removed, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 4 medium silverbeet leaves (320g), stems removed, chopped coarsely

steps: Rinse split peas under cold water until water runs clear; drain.
Heat ghee in a large frying pan; cook onion, garlic, ginger and chilli, stirring, until onion softens. Add seeds and spices; cook, stirring, until fragrant.
Transfer onion mixture to 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; stir in tomatoes, stock, the water, sugar and split peas. Cook, covered, on low, 10 hours.
Add silver beet, stir until wilted; season to taste.

recipes.title: How to make classic chocolate brownies in your slow cooker
recipes.description: Chocolate brownies just got easier!
steps: Line a rectangular slow cooker with two layers of baking paper; do not preheat.
Finely chop 200g (6½oz) of the chocolate; cut remaining 100g (3oz) into chunks.

Microwave chopped butter in a large microwave-safe bowl
until melted; stir in sugar. Microwave for a further 1 minute or until very hot. Stir in the finely chopped chocolate until chocolate melts. Whisk in egg in two batches; stir in flour.
Pour batter into lined slow cooker. Smooth surface to remove any air bubbles. Scatter with reserved chocolate chunks and walnuts.
Heat slow cooker on LOW. Cover slow cooker insert with a clean tea towel, then replace cooker lid; cook for 3 hours. Turn off cooker; leave brownie to cool for 20 minutes in cooker, covered.
Transfer brownie to a cutting board; cut into 24 pieces.

recipes.title: Slow cooker potato bake
recipes.description: Your favourite side dish just got a whole lot easier.

ingredients.name: coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

ingredients.name: gram packet dried chicken noodle soup mix
ingredients.raw: 50 gram packet dried chicken noodle soup mix

steps: Heat oil in a large frying pan; cook leek and bacon, stirring, for 5 minutes or until leek softens. Remove from heat; stir in parsley.
Layer one third of the potato in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; top with half the leek mixture. Repeat layering with remaining potato and leek, finishing with potato layer.
Combine cream, milk, mustard and soup mix in a large jug, pour over potato; sprinkle with combined cheddar and parmesan. Cook, covered, on low, about 6 hours.

recipes.title: Slow cooker vegetable minestrone
recipes.description: A simple soup made even easier.

ingredients.name: grams canned cannellini beans, drained, rinsed
ingredients.raw: 400 grams (12½ ounces) canned cannellini beans, drained, rinsed

ingredients.name: grams green beans, trimmed, cut into 3cm lengths
ingredients.raw: 150 grams (4½ ounces) green beans, trimmed, cut into 3cm (1¼-inch) lengths
1/2 cup loosely packed small fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves

steps: Place garlic, onion, celery, carrot, stock, paste and tomatoes in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 6 hours.
Add cannellini beans and enough of the water to make a thick soup consistency. Cook, covered, on low, for 2 hours. Season to taste.
Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large saucepan of boiling salted water until just tender, adding green beans and peas for the last 2 minutes of cooking time; drain.
Divide pasta, green beans, peas and cabbage among serving bowls; ladle soup mixture into bowls. Serve minestrone topped with parmesan and parsley.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker loaded veg ‘bolognese’ baked potatoes
recipes.description: Vegie-packed and delicious.

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup coarsely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Place onion, garlic, eggplant, capsicum, celery, carrot, zucchini, tomatoes, paste, rosemary and stock in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; stir to combine.
Wrap each potato, separately, in foil; place on top of vegetable mixture in cooker. Cook, covered, on low for 8 hours.
Carefully remove potatoes; when cool enough to handle, discard foil. Season vegetable 'bolognese' to taste.
Cut lengthways down centre of each potato; press down lightly on a board to open potato slightly. Place potatoes on serving plates; top with vegetable 'bolognese' and parsley.

recipes.title: Slow cooker tomato soup with basil dumplings
recipes.description: Soak up this rich and delicious tomato soup with tender, herby dumplings.

ingredients.name: kilogram over-ripe roma tomatoes, cored
ingredients.raw: 1 kilogram (2 pounds) over-ripe roma (egg) tomatoes, cored

ingredients.name: fresh long red chilli, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh long red chilli, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: drained roasted red capsicum , chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 cup (200g) drained roasted red capsicum (bell pepper), chopped coarsely
1/4 cup loosely packed fresh small basil leaves or micro basil

steps: Place tomatoes, garlic, onion, chilli, celery and capsicum in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 7 hours.
Cool soup for 10 minutes. Using a stick blender, blend soup until smooth. Season to taste.
Make basil dumpling mixture: Place flour in a medium bowl; using your fingertips, rub in butter. Stir in egg, pesto and enough of the milk to make a soft, sticky dough.
Carefully drop level tablespoons of the basil dumpling mixture, about 2cm (¾-inch) apart, on top of soup. Cook, covered, on low, for 1 hour or until dumplings are firm to touch and cooked through.
Serve soup with dumplings and sprinkled with basil. Top with halve mixed baby tomatoes, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb chops
recipes.description: Cooked low and slow for plenty of flavour.

ingredients.name: gram packaged french onion soup mix ·
ingredients.raw: 80 gram packaged french onion soup mix ·

ingredients.name: coarsely chopped
ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, coarsely chopped

steps: Trim excess fat from lamb. Toss lamb in flour to coat, shake off excess. Heat oil in a large frying pan, cook lamb, in batches, until browned.

Place 4 lamb chops into a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Sprinkle one-third of the soup mix then one-third of the leek and celery over the chops. Repeat layering with remaining lamb, soup mix, leek and celery. Pour stock into cooker. Cook, covered, on low, 6 hours.
Remove lamb from cooker, cover to keep warm. Skim fat from surface of sauce, season to taste. Serve lamb and sauce sprinkled with parsley.

recipes.title: Shepherd’s pie in the slow cooker
recipes.description: Tender, delicate lamb with a buttery mash top.
steps: Combine carrot, celery, onion, garlic, herbs, paste, sauce and stock in a 5-litre (20-cup) slow cooker. Add lamb; turn to coat in mixture. Cook, covered, on high for 2 hours. Reduce to low; cook for 8 hours.

Remove lamb from cooker; shred meat coarsely, discard fat and bones. Discard herbs from cooker. Return lamb to cooker with peas and spinach. Blend cornflour and 1 tablespoon water in a small cup. Stir into mixture in the cooker; cook, uncovered, on high, for 20 minutes or until thickened. Season to taste.
Meanwhile, boil, steam or microwave potato until tender; drain. Mash potato with butter and hot milk until smooth. Season to taste.
Preheat grill.

Transfer lamb to a 2.5-litre (10-cup) ovenproof dish. Spoon potato evenly over lamb mixture; lightly rough up  the surface with a fork. Sprinkle with cheddar. Grill pie for 5 minutes or  until potato topping is browned lightly.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker apricot chicken
recipes.description: Set and forget!

ingredients.name: grams packet cream of chicken simmer soup mix
ingredients.raw: 45 grams (1½-ounce) packet cream of chicken simmer soup mix

ingredients.name: medium leek , white part only, sliced thickly
ingredients.raw: 1 medium leek (350g), white part only, sliced thickly

steps: Place soup mix in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Gradually whisk in nectar until smooth.
Remove and discard fat and skin from chicken. Place chicken, leek, garlic and apricots in cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 4 hours. Season to taste.
Sprinkle chicken with almonds and micro herbs, if using.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb chops with ratatouille
recipes.description: Simply set and forget.

ingredients.name: medium red onion , cut into wedges
ingredients.raw: 1 medium red onion (170g), cut into wedges

ingredients.name: medium yellow capsicum , chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 medium yellow capsicum (bell pepper) (200g), chopped coarsely

steps: Place flour in a large bowl; season.

Heat oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting. Coat lamb in flour; shake off excess. Cook lamb, in batches, for 5 minutes each side, or until well browned. Return lamb to cooker.
Add eggplant, zucchini, onion, capsicum, anchovies, garlic and passata to cooker; top with tomatoes. Cook, covered, on low, for 6 hours.
Stir in olives; season to taste.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker pork and lemongrass curry
recipes.description: Hearty, warming and packed full of flavour, this delicious pork and lemongrass curry is the easiest way you'll make an authentic Asian style curry at home. Simply pop all the ingredients in the slow-cooker, and let the magic happen.

ingredients.name: slow-cooker pork and lemon grass curry
ingredients.raw: Slow-cooker pork and lemon grass curry

ingredients.name: x 10cm sticks fresh lemon grass , chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 2 x 10cm (4-inch) sticks fresh lemon grass (40g), chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: gram piece fresh galangal, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 20 gram piece fresh galangal, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: fresh small red thai chilli, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh small red thai (serrano) chilli, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: fresh kaffir lime leaves, shredded thinly
ingredients.raw: 3 fresh kaffir lime leaves, shredded thinly
1/2 cup loosely packed fresh coriander (cilantro) leaves

steps: Blend or process lemon grass, garlic, galangal, chilli, spices, lime leaves, onion and the water until mixture is smooth.
Heat oil in a medium frying pan; cook lemon grass paste, stirring, for 5 minutes or until fragrant.
Transfer lemon grass paste to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; stir in pork, coconut milk and eggplant. Cook, covered, on low, for 4 hours.
Add carrots; cook, covered, on low, for 2 hours. Stir in sauce and juice; season to taste. Serve curry topped with coriander.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb cassoulet
recipes.description: This tender and moist slow-cooker lamb cassoulet is the perfect option for dinner tonight - warm, hearty and delicious.

ingredients.name: gram boned lamb shoulder, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 900 gram boned lamb shoulder, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoon finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Discard the rind from the speck. Cut speck into 3cm pieces.
Heat half the oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat; cook sausages and speck, turning, until browned all over. Transfer to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.
Cook lamb in same frying pan, in batches, over medium-high heat, until browned all over. Transfer to cooker. Drain fat from pan.

Heat remaining oil in same frying pan over medium heat; cook onion and bay leaf, stirring, for 5 minutes or until onion softens. Add garlic; cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant. Transfer to cooker.
Add tomatoes, paste, beans and the water to cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Just before serving, sprinkle cassoulet with parsley and, if you like, lightly toasted breadcrumbs.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker red pork curry
recipes.description: A fragrant red curry with slow-cooked pork that will melt in your mouth.

ingredients.name: fresh kaffir lime leaves, shredded finely
ingredients.raw: 3 fresh kaffir lime leaves, shredded finely

ingredients.name: kilogram rindless boneless pork belly, chopped coarsely
ingredients.raw: 1.5 kilogram rindless boneless pork belly, chopped coarsely

ingredients.name: loosely packed fresh thai basil leaves
ingredients.raw: 1 cup loosely packed fresh thai basil leaves

ingredients.name: fresh long red chillies, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 2 fresh long red chillies, sliced thinly

ingredients.name: fresh kaffir lime leaf, shredded finely, extra
ingredients.raw: 1 fresh kaffir lime leaf, shredded finely, extra

steps: Combine coconut cream, stock, paste, sauce and lime leaves in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; add pork and kumara. Cook, covered, on high, 4 hours.
Skim fat from surface. Stir in beans; cook, covered, on high, 30 minutes. Season to taste.
Serve topped with basil, chilli and extra shredded lime leaf.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb with kale and pomegranate
recipes.description: This sweet, meltingly-tender lamb recipe makes a show-stopping dinner-party dish.

ingredients.name: boneless lamb shoulder, cut into 10cm chunks
ingredients.raw: 1.2 kg (2½ pounds) boneless lamb shoulder, cut into 10cm (4-inch) chunks

ingredients.name: medium kale leaves , trimmed, shredded coarsely
ingredients.raw: 4 medium kale leaves (140g), trimmed, shredded coarsely

steps: Combine lamb with spices and chilli in a large bowl; season. Heat half the oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on sear (HIGH) setting; cook lamb, in batches, turning, for 10 minutes, or until well browned. Remove from cooker.

Meanwhile, cut whole pomegranate in half. Hold pomegranate, cut-side down over a sieve into a medium bowl and hit the back of the pomegranate with a wooden spoon to release the juice and seeds. Reserve ¼ cup (60ml) juice and ⅓ cup seeds separately. Refrigerate seeds until required.

Heat remaining oil in cooker on sauté (MEDIUM) setting; cook onion, garlic and ginger, stirring, for 5 minutes or until onion softens. Stir in stock, maple syrup, molasses and reserved pomegranate juice. Return lamb to cooker; bring to the boil.
Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Add kale to cooker; cook, covered, on high for 5 minutes or until kale is wilted. Season to taste.
Serve lamb topped with reserved pomegranate seeds and almonds.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb kleftiko with potatoes
recipes.description: Let the slow cooker work its magic and come home to a meal that's ready when you are.

ingredients.name: kilogram red- and white-skinned baby potatoes
ingredients.raw: 1 kilogram red- and white-skinned baby potatoes

ingredients.name: rigani or dried oregano
ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon rigani (see tips) or dried oregano
1/3 cup loosely packed fresh oregano leaves

steps: Heat half the oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat; cook lamb, turning, for 20 minutes or until well browned.
Meanwhile, heat remaining oil in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker on Sauté (Medium) setting; cook potatoes, onion, bay leaves and cinnamon, stirring, for 10 minutes or until browned.
Place lamb on top of potatoes in cooker. Combine wine, stock, rind, juice, rigani and garlic in a small jug; pour over lamb. Cook, covered, on Low, for 9 hours or until meat is falling off the bone.
Serve lamb with potatoes and a little cooking liquid. Sprinkle with fresh oregano.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker coq au vin
recipes.description: Breathe in and smell the aromas...
steps: Trim green ends from onions, leaving about 4cm (1½ inches) of stem attached; trim roots leaving onions intact. Heat half the oil in a large frying pan; cook onions, stirring, until browned all over, remove from pan. Add bacon, mushrooms and garlic to same pan; cook, stirring, until bacon is crisp, remove from pan.
Cut chicken into 12 pieces. Heat remaining oil in same pan; cook chicken, in batches, until browned all over; drain on paper towel. Add wine to same pan; bring to the boil, stirring.
Place chicken in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker with onions, bacon and mushroom mixture, carrot, herbs, stock, wine mixture and paste. Cook, covered, on low, about 7 hours.
Stir in blended cornflour and the water; cook, covered, on high, for 20 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly. Season to taste.

recipes.title: Creamy rice pudding with cinnamon sugar
steps: Combine rice, milk, sugar and rind in 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Halve vanilla bean lengthways, scrape seeds into cooker, add vanilla bean.
Cook, covered, on low, 6 hours, stirring twice, or until rice is tender. Discard vanilla bean and rind.
Combine extra sugar and cinnamon in small bowl, sprinkle over pudding.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker chicken hot and sour soup
recipes.description: We love slow cookers - simply pop all your ingredients into the cooker and come back to a warming, flavour-packed dish. This zesty hot and sour chicken soup tastes absolutely amazing after several hours of slow-cooking.

ingredients.name: kilogram whole chicken, fat and skin removed
ingredients.raw: 1.6 kilogram whole chicken, fat and skin removed

ingredients.name: fresh long red chillies, thinly sliced, plus extra for garnish
ingredients.raw: 2 fresh long red chillies, thinly sliced, plus extra for garnish

ingredients.name: gram canned straw mushrooms, rinsed, drained
ingredients.raw: 425 gram canned straw mushrooms, rinsed, drained

ingredients.name: baby buk choy, trimmed, coarsely chopped
ingredients.raw: 2 (300g) baby buk choy, trimmed, coarsely chopped
1/2 cup firmly packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Place chicken, carrot, celery, onion and the water in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Carefully remove chicken from cooker; shred the meat coarsely using two forks. Discard bones. Strain cooking liquid through muslin-lined sieve or colander and discard solids.
Return strained liquid to cooker with lime leaves, lemon grass, ginger, chilli, juice, sauce, sugar, mushrooms and chicken. Cook, covered, on high for 30 minutes. Stir in buk choy.

Meanwhile, place noodles in a medium heatproof bowl and cover with boiling water. Stand 10 minutes or until softened, drain. Divide noodles among serving bowls. Ladle hot soup over noodles. Serve sprinkled with coriander and fresh sliced chilli, if you like.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker seafood in romesco sauce
steps: Place octopus, tomatoes, garlic, chilli, paprika,capsicum and vinegar in a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; cook, covered, on low, 4 hours.

Shell and devein prawns, leaving tails intact. Add prawns, mussels and ground almonds to cooker; cook, covered, stirring occasionally, on high, for 20 minutes or until prawns change colour and mussels open. Season to taste.
Serve sprinkled with herbs.

recipes.title: Slow-cooker lamb & eggplant curry
recipes.description: A rich, fragrant and warming slow-cooked lamb and eggplant curry that will melt in your mouth.

ingredients.name: fresh long red chillies, sliced thinly
ingredients.raw: 3 fresh long red chillies, sliced thinly
1/4 cup lightly packed fresh coriander leaves

steps: Cut eggplants into 3cm thick slices. Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat; cook eggplant, in batches, until browned. Transfer to a 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker.
Heat another tablespoon of the oil in same frying pan over medium heat; cook lamb, in batches, until browned. Transfer to the slow cooker.

Heat remaining oil in same frying pan over medium heat; cook onion, stirring, for 5 minutes or until softened. Add garlic, ginger, chilli and spices; cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant. Stir in tomatoes and stock; bring to the boil. Transfer to the slow cooker. Cook, covered, on low, for 8 hours.
Dry-fry seeds, nuts and coconut until fragrant but not coloured; cool. Blend or process until finely ground. Stir nut mixture into curry. Season to taste.
Serve curry topped with yoghurt and coriander.

recipes.title: Sticky date and fig steamed pudding

ingredients.name: seeded dried dates, finely chopped
ingredients.raw: 2 cup (300g) seeded dried dates, finely chopped

ingredients.name: firmly packed light brown sugar
ingredients.raw: 1 cup (220g) firmly packed light brown sugar
3/4 cup (165g) firmly packed light brown sugar

steps: Combine fruit, the water, sugar and butter in medium saucepan; stir over heat until butter melts and sugar dissolves. Bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, 5 minutes. Transfer mixture to large heatproof bowl, stir in soda; cool 10 minutes.
Stir eggs and sifted dry ingredients into fruit mixture.
Grease 2-litre (8-cup) pudding steamer; spoon mixture into steamer. Top with pleated baking paper and foil; secure with kitchen string or lid.

Place steamer in 4.5-litre (18-cup) slow cooker; pour enough boiling water into cooker to come halfway up side of steamer. Cook, covered, on high, 5 hours, replenishing with boiling water as necessary to maintain level.
Remove pudding from cooker. Stand 10 minutes before turning onto plate.
Meanwhile, make butterscotch sauce; stir ingredients in medium saucepan over heat, without boiling, until sugar  dissolves; bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, 2 minutes.
Serve pudding with butterscotch sauce, and a dollop of thick cream, if you like.

recipes.title: Mince & rice stuffed capsicums
recipes.description: Stuff your face with these!

ingredients.name: rinsed, drained
ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (100g) white long-grain rice, rinsed, drained
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Heat oil in large frying pan, add onion and garlic; cook, stirring, until onion softens. Add pork and veal and half the thyme; cook, stirring, until browned.
Add sauce, stock, rice and parsley; bring to the boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, about 15 minutes or until rice is almost tender, stirring occasionally, season to taste.
Meanwhile, oil small baking dish. Cut tops from capsicums, reserve as lids. Scoop out seeds and membranes. Fill capsicums with mince mixture; stand upright in baking dish.

Combine breadcrumbs, cheese, rind and remaining thyme in small bowl. Sprinkle over filling; place capsicum tops in baking dish. Bake capsicums, uncovered, about 1 hour or until rice is soft. Top stuffed capsicums with lids to serve.

recipes.title: Steph de Sousa’s air fryer steak
recipes.description: This succulent steak dinner for one can easily be scaled up to feed the fam!

ingredients.name: your favourite cut of boneless steak 2.5cm thick
ingredients.raw: Your favourite cut of boneless steak 2.5cm thick

steps: Remove steak from the fridge and unwrap 30 minutes before cooking so the chill is gone.
I don’t usually preheat the air fryer but for this recipe I do! Set the temperature to 200°C and the timer for 5 minutes.
Prep the steaks: Splash some olive oil on that bad boy, then shower it generously with salt and pepper, and rub in.
Toss your steak into the heated air fryer. Set the timer for 4 minutes. Now, flip that steak. Then, back into the air fryer it goes for another 4 minutes.
This timing is for medium-rare. For a medium steak, add 1 minute each side; for medium-well, add 2 minutes each side; and for well-done, add 3 minutes each side.

Alright, your steak's cooked. Now, quickly wrap it up in some foil with a little dollop of butter. Let it rest for 5 minutes. This lets all those tasty juices soak back into the steak, making it extra juicy and delicious.
Serve it up and enjoy your scrumptious steak with a side of onion rings!

recipes.title: Steph de Sousa’s slow cooker potato bake
recipes.description: A spud-tacular side dish!
steps: Let’s get this creamy party started! In a bowl, whisk together the cream, milk and soup mix until it’s all smooth and looking irresistible. This is where the magic begins!
Now for the fun part—layering up the goodness. Start by placing one-third of your sliced potatoes in the bottom of your 5.5L (22-cup) slow cooker. Think of it as the first layer of a flavour tower!
On top of those potatoes, sprinkle half each of the diced ham, green onion, capsicum, mushrooms and a generous ½ cup of cheese.
Time to double up! Repeat the layering with another third of the potato and the rest of the ham, green onion, capsicum, mushrooms and another ½ cup of cheese. Now it’s getting cheesy and exciting!

Top it all off with the last third of your potatoes. Pour that creamy mix you whisked up earlier all over the top, making sure everything is coated and ready to mingle. Then, sprinkle the remaining cheese like a final blanket of comfort.

Cover the top with a sheet of baking paper, extending it beyond the rim of the slow cooker. Pop the lid on your slow cooker, set it to LOW, and let it work its magic for 6 hours. The aroma that’ll fill your kitchen? Pure, creamy bliss!
Once it’s done, give it a 15-minute rest before serving. This little break lets the flavours settle and makes the potato bake even more delicious—if that’s even possible!

recipes.title: Teriyaki chicken bowl
recipes.description: This 15-minute meal is a midweek winner.

ingredients.name: chicken thigh fillets, cut in half
ingredients.raw: 6 (1kg) chicken thigh fillets, cut in half

ingredients.name: green onions, cut into 3cm lengths, tips shredded
ingredients.raw: 3 green onions, cut into 3cm lengths, tips shredded

ingredients.name: steamed sushi rice, shelled edamame, shredded carrot and toasted sesame seeds, to serve
ingredients.raw: steamed sushi rice, shelled edamame, shredded carrot and toasted sesame seeds, to serve

steps: Heat half the oil in a large non-stick frying pan or wok over high heat. Add the chicken, cook for 3-4 minutes each side or until well browned and cooked through. Transfer chicken to a plate. Cover to keep warm.
Meanwhile, combine cornflour and ¼ cup (60ml) cold water in a small cup. Stir to dissolve.

In same pan, heat remaining oil over high heat. Cook onion and garlic until fragrant but not coloured. Add ginger, sauces, sesame oil and cornflour mixture; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes or until mixture has boiled and thickened.
Return the chicken and any resting juices to the pan, simmer until chicken is heated through.
Divide rice, edamame, carrot and teriyaki chicken mixture between serving bowls. Top with shredded green onion tips and toasted sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Julie Goodwin’s seafood pie
recipes.description: Classic comfort food made easy.

ingredients.name: large brown onion, very finely sliced
ingredients.raw: 1 large brown onion, very finely sliced

ingredients.name: salt, plus extra for the potato
ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon salt, plus extra for the potato

ingredients.name: ground white pepper, plus extra for the potato
ingredients.raw: ¼ teaspoon ground white pepper, plus extra for the potato

ingredients.name: firm fleshed, skinless white fish, cubed into 3cm pieces
ingredients.raw: 500g firm fleshed, skinless white fish, cubed into 3cm pieces

ingredients.name: green king prawns, shelled and deveined, cut into thirds
ingredients.raw: 300g green (uncooked) king prawns, shelled and deveined, cut into thirds

ingredients.name: dill, leaves finely chopped
ingredients.raw: ½ bunch dill, leaves finely chopped

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C fan-forced.
Place the potatoes in a saucepan of salted cold water. Boil for about 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender.

While the potatoes are cooking, heat 50g of the butter in a large pan over a medium heat and sauté the onion for about 5 minutes or until soft. Add the flour and stir until well combined. Add 2 cups of the milk a little at a time, stirring continuously, ensuring that the flour cooks after each addition. Stir through the mustard, salt and pepper.
